project.name: dotfiles
project.description: Opinionated Bash/Zsh dotfiles for reproducible terminal environments across WSL, Linux, and macOS.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that runs mise-driven lint (ruff, mypy, shellcheck, shfmt) on push/PR to main or develop; the former pytest job was moved to the local pre-push git hook to save CI runner time.
nodes.languageNotes: Tool versions are pinned once in mise.toml, the same SSOT used by local `mise run lint`, so CI and dev machines cannot drift.

nodes.name: cli-package.yml
nodes.summary: Node.js CI/CD pipeline scoped to packages/my-cli — runs test/coverage, lint/typecheck, bundle size check, e2e tests, and a release job that publishes on tag/version bump.

nodes.name: project-board-sync.yml
nodes.summary: Syncs GitHub Project board card columns automatically as PRs move through opened, review-approved, and merged states, guarded by a required PROJECT_BOARD_PAT check on canonical PRs.

nodes.name: test.yml
nodes.summary: Shell-lint-only GitHub Actions workflow running `mise run lint-sh` (shellcheck + shfmt diff) on push/PR, replacing a previous inline shellcheck loop that silently swallowed failures.

nodes.name: .gh-issue-defaults.yml
nodes.summary: Repo SSOT read by the gh:issue-create skill's auto-labelling step, mapping conventional-commit title prefixes (feat, fix, refactor, etc.) to GitHub labels and controlling milestone assignment.

nodes.name: .markdownlint.json
nodes.summary: markdownlint rule configuration disabling line-length (MD013), ordered-list numbering, blank-line, and inline-HTML rules to fit the repo's Markdown authoring style.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Repo-root agent guidance covering package-manager rules, golden rules, immutable constraints, and a context map pointing to module-level AGENTS.md files.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Primary Claude Code guidance document for the dotfiles repo: setup/lint/test commands, the bash/zsh/shell-common architecture, critical POSIX/interactive-guard rules, and a pointer to the knowledge-graph codebase map.

nodes.name: PORTS.md
nodes.summary: Central port registry (SSOT) documenting the decade-block allocation formula and reserved port ranges across the user's projects.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and onboarding guide covering features, quick-start installation, directory structure, git-crypt secret management, development/lint commands, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: REFACTORING_SETUP_SH.md
nodes.summary: Historical design document recording the SOLID-principle refactor of setup.sh — config extraction, function separation, an environments.conf SSOT, before/after metrics, and a migration path for future phases.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: Optional post-setup installer that configures Claude IDE integration and PostgreSQL settings, supplementing the required ./setup.sh shell-environment bootstrap.

nodes.name: create_symlink
nodes.summary: Creates (or replaces) a symlink for a given source/target pair, backing up any pre-existing real file before linking.

nodes.name: mise.toml
nodes.summary: mise task-runner configuration defining tool versions, environment variables, and the lint/fix/test task SSOT (lint, lint-py, lint-sh, lint-docs, fix, fix-py, fix-sh) shared by CI and local dev.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project configuration (hatchling/setuptools build, dependency groups, pytest, mypy, and ruff lint/format settings) for the repo's Python tooling and tests.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Auto-generated pinned dependency lockfile (via `uv pip compile`) covering rich, markdown-it-py, mdurl, and pygments used by the repo's Python tooling.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Required shell-environment setup orchestrator that canonicalizes the dotfiles root and delegates to bash/, zsh/, git/, and claude/ sub-setup scripts to create symlinks and set required environment variables.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Module-level agent guidance for aws/ describing its single responsibility (internal-PC AWS Bedrock + Claude bootstrap), SSOT ownership rules, execution flow, and the settings.json merge policy.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step onboarding guide for internal-PC AWS Bedrock setup — running aws/setup.sh, editing aws.local.sh, `aws sso login`, installing OTel telemetry, restarting Claude Code, and running aws/diagnose.sh, plus a troubleshooting reverse-index.

nodes.name: diagnose.sh
nodes.summary: Read-only diagnostic script (issue #677) that checks whether the aws/setup.sh and aws/install-otel-managed-settings.sh bootstrap completed correctly — proxy certs, AWS CLI, Bedrock env vars, ~/.aws/config, Claude settings, OTel managed-settings, and SSO session state — reporting PASS/FAIL/WARN per item.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps the whole script body in a subshell so it stays safe to copy-paste directly into a terminal.

nodes.name: _env_registered
nodes.summary: Checks whether a given environment variable is registered in the Claude settings.json env block, used by later PASS/FAIL checks.

nodes.name: install-otel-managed-settings.sh
nodes.summary: Installer that writes /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json with the internal Samsung OTLP telemetry endpoint and a dynamic user.id derived from `aws sts get-caller-identity`; requires internal-PC mode, AWS CLI, prior SSO login, and sudo, and is idempotent across re-runs.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Internal-PC AWS Bedrock + Claude bootstrap script (issues #677, #687) that seeds aws.local.sh and ~/.aws/config, deep-merges a Bedrock overlay into a real ~/.claude/settings.json file via jq (working around a settings.local.json deep-merge bug), and archives legacy local settings; a no-op on external/public PCs.

nodes.name: _aws_setup_mode
nodes.summary: Reads and normalizes the ~/.dotfiles-setup-mode file to determine whether the current PC is in internal or external/public mode, gating the rest of the script.

nodes.name: _seed_file
nodes.summary: Seeds a target config file from a template on first run without overwriting a user's existing edits, used for aws.local.sh and similar local-override files.

nodes.name: _merge_claude_settings_json
nodes.summary: Deep-merges the dotfiles SSOT claude/settings.json with the Bedrock overlay example via jq into a real (non-symlinked) ~/.claude/settings.json, working around a Claude Code settings.local.json deep-merge bug (#687) that silently dropped ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL and caused 400 invalid-model errors.

nodes.name: _archive_legacy_settings_local
nodes.summary: Archives a legacy settings.local.json left over from before the real-file settings.json merge strategy, preventing stale overrides from re-applying.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Module guidance for bash/ covering operational commands, the file-loading implementation pattern, the mandatory interactive guard, golden rules, and testing strategy for the bash-specific entry point.

nodes.name: main.bash
nodes.summary: Bash-specific shell entry point that guards against loading under zsh, non-interactive shells, or Codex CLI environments, then bootstraps interactive bash configuration (help functions, script-name resolution) sourced from shell-common/.

nodes.name: should_skip_init
nodes.summary: Decides whether bash initialization should be skipped, based on DOTFILES_FORCE_INIT, an explicit skip flag, non-interactive shell detection, or a detected Codex CLI environment.

nodes.name: profile.bash
nodes.summary: Minimal ~/.bash_profile replacement that sources ~/.bashrc so interactive-shell settings also apply to login shells.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Required bash-specific setup script that exports DOTFILES_BASH_DIR and SHELL_COMMON, symlinks ~/.bashrc and ~/.bash_profile, backs up pre-existing real files, and cleans up broken zsh plugin symlinks left over from prior installs.

nodes.name: create_symlink
nodes.summary: Creates or replaces a symlink for a source/target pair, backing up any existing real file first, mirroring the same helper used by the root install.sh.

nodes.name: _cleanup_broken_zsh_plugins
nodes.summary: Detects and removes dangling zsh plugin symlinks left behind by earlier setup runs so a bash-only environment doesn't accumulate broken zsh artifacts.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent-facing reference for the claude/ module: documents the skills SSOT and its 4-way (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI) entry-level symlink scheme, the settings.json tracked-SSOT model, and the two-entry-path plugin manifest sync system.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Global instructions symlinked into every Claude Code account: defines the Advisor/Worker model-delegation policy (main session judges, opus sub-agents implement) and mandates the Karpathy coding guidelines as a standing behavioral baseline.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Setup and customization guide for the claude/ directory, covering the tracked settings.json SSOT, the Internal-PC ANTHROPIC_* override file, and SKILL.md single-line description authoring rules.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Tracked SSOT for Claude Code's runtime settings: registers SessionStart/Stop/PostToolUse hooks, points statusLine at the dotfiles status-line script, and enables the project's plugin set (superpowers, ralph-loop, codex, understand-anything).

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Installer that symlinks settings.json, statusline-command.sh, skills, docs, and global memory into ~/.claude (or per-account config dirs), plus one-off legacy-layout migrations for statusline path, plugin paths, and stop-hook installation.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of guarded one-off `_migrate_*` functions that detect and repair stale symlink layouts from earlier dotfiles versions — a pattern for backward-compatible setup scripts.

nodes.name: statusline-command.sh
nodes.summary: Renders the Claude Code status line (time, model, project/branch, git status, token usage) from the JSON payload piped in on stdin, consolidating what would be several jq calls into one pass for performance.

nodes.name: _migrate_legacy_statusline_command
nodes.summary: One-off migration that detects and fixes a stale statusline-command.sh symlink layout left over from an earlier setup.sh version.

nodes.name: _migrate_legacy_plugin_paths
nodes.summary: One-off migration that rewrites legacy Claude plugin directory paths to the current shared-plugins symlink scheme.

nodes.name: _migrate_install_gh_issue_flow_stop_hook
nodes.summary: Installs or upgrades the gh:issue-flow Stop hook entry in settings.json for accounts still missing it.

nodes.name: _print_stale_bind_mount_sudoers_hint
nodes.summary: Detects leftover /etc/sudoers.d bind-mount files from the pre-#575 setup era and prints manual cleanup instructions.

nodes.name: _single_account_ensure_link
nodes.summary: Ensures a single required symlink exists for the single-account (Internal PC) setup mode, creating or repairing it as needed.

nodes.name: kubernetes-architect_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference for the Kubernetes Architect agent, covering platform expertise, GitOps continuous delivery, cloud-native security, service mesh, and OpenGitOps principles.

nodes.name: network-engineer_KO.md

nodes.name: service-mesh-expert_KO.md

nodes.name: terraform-specialist_KO.md

nodes.name: context-restore_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference for the vendored code-refactoring plugin's context-restore command, describing semantic-vector-based session/context rehydration: input parameters, retrieval/ranking strategy, and cross-project knowledge-transfer workflows.

nodes.name: refactor-clean_KO.md
nodes.summary: Extensive Korean-translated reference for the refactor-clean command, walking through before/after examples for single-responsibility, open/closed, modular architecture, CI-integrated static analysis (Sourcery, Semgrep, CodeQL), exception handling, and O(n²)-to-O(n) algorithmic cleanup.

nodes.name: tech-debt_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference for the tech-debt command, covering technical-debt inventory (code/architecture/test/docs/infra debt), impact assessment, metrics dashboards, prioritized remediation plans, and prevention strategies.

nodes.name: ai-engineer_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference for the vendored llm-application-dev plugin's AI Engineer agent, covering LLM integration/model ops, advanced RAG systems, agent orchestration frameworks, vector search/embeddings, and production AI system deployment.

nodes.name: prompt-engineer_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference for the Prompt Engineer agent, covering core prompting rules, advanced techniques, model-specific optimization strategies, production prompt systems, and evaluation/testing frameworks.

nodes.name: vector-database-engineer_KO.md
nodes.summary: Concise Korean-translated reference for the Vector Database Engineer agent, summarizing its capabilities, when-to-use triggers, recommended workflow, and best practices for embedding/vector-search infrastructure.

nodes.name: ai-assistant_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference for the vendored llm-application-dev plugin's ai-assistant command, covering conversational-AI architecture, NLU/NLP pipelines, dialogue-flow design, context management, and LLM provider integration with fallback and function calling.

nodes.name: langchain-agent_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference for the langchain-agent command, covering LangGraph state management, agent pattern types, memory systems, RAG pipelines, tool design, and production deployment/cost-optimization strategy.

nodes.name: prompt-optimize_KO.md
nodes.summary: Concise Korean-translated reference for the prompt-optimize command, summarizing its purpose, step-by-step optimization workflow, deliverable output format, and usage guidelines.

nodes.name: chain-of-thought_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference sheet on chain-of-thought prompting: core techniques, advanced patterns, domain-specific CoT templates, performance optimization, evaluation metrics, and common pitfalls.

nodes.name: few-shot-learning_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference on few-shot prompting: example-selection strategies (semantic similarity, diversity, difficulty, error-based), formatting consistency, context-window token budgeting, and edge-case handling.

nodes.name: prompt-optimization_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference on systematic prompt optimization: baselining, iterative improvement workflow, A/B testing, token/latency/accuracy optimization strategies, and versioning/rollback practices.

nodes.name: prompt-templates_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference on prompt template architecture: basic/conditional/modular template patterns, template inheritance registries, variable validation, caching, and multi-turn conversation/state templates.

nodes.name: system-prompts_KO.md
nodes.summary: Korean-translated reference on system-prompt design: recommended structure, a role-based pattern library (customer support, data analyst, content editor), dynamic role adaptation, and common pitfalls in testing system prompts.

nodes.name: plugin-sync-session.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code SessionStart/Stop hook that watches the shared plugin manifest SSOT (known_marketplaces.json / installed_plugins.json) for changes made via the `/plugin` slash-command UI path, and drives plugin-sync.sh to add or remove entries symmetrically since that hook only fires for the CLI path.
nodes.languageNotes: Snapshot-diff pattern: hashes + key sets are stashed at SessionStart and compared at Stop to detect additions and removals without a dedicated hook event for slash commands.

nodes.name: plugin-sync.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code PostToolUse hook for `claude plugin ...` Bash invocations; keeps claude/plugin/{marketplaces,plugins}.json (and the private company/ nested repo) in sync with the shared ground-truth plugin manifest and auto-commits (and pushes when on a protected branch) any resulting diff.

nodes.name: post-bash-dispatch.sh
nodes.summary: Single front-door PostToolUse:Bash dispatcher that reads the hook envelope once and routes to post-gh-pr-create.sh or plugin-sync.sh only when the command cheaply looks relevant, cutting duplicate hook-process spawns per Bash call.

nodes.name: post-gh-pr-create.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code PostToolUse hook for `gh pr create` that parses the created PR URL and syncs the GitHub project board: sets the PR card to "In review" (with a poll/retry to absorb GitHub's auto-add race) and moves linked issues to "In progress".

nodes.name: session-start-pc-context.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code SessionStart hook that injects the current PC's dotfiles setup-mode (public/internal/external) and hostname as additionalContext, so a fresh session knows which per-machine mode it is running in without relying on shared CLAUDE.md or model memory.

nodes.name: session-start-plugin-path-normalize.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code SessionStart hook that rewrites plugin installLocation/installPath fields in the shared plugin manifest to the spelling of the currently active $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, working around Claude Code's literal string-prefix validation across a symlinked multi-account plugin store.

nodes.name: session-start-settings-drift.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code SessionStart hook that compares the live settings.json `.hooks` block against the dotfiles SSOT (claude/settings.json) and warns via additionalContext when they've drifted, since the live file is a real copy that only updates on re-running setup.sh.

nodes.name: skill_step_catalog.yml
nodes.summary: SSOT catalog for skill_completion_guard.py listing which multi-step SKILL.md workflows (gh-issue-implement, gh-pr, gh-commit) have mechanical step-skip protection and the literal step-marker IDs each must emit before the guard allows the turn to end.

nodes.name: _snapshot
nodes.summary: Hashes the marketplace/plugin manifest SSOT files and extracts their key sets (with a macOS shasum fallback) into a compact JSON object used to detect drift between SessionStart and Stop.

nodes.name: _extract_target
nodes.summary: Parses the first non-flag argument following a subcommand keyword out of a `claude plugin` command string, so flags placed between the subcommand and target are not mistaken for it.

nodes.name: _commit_if_changed
nodes.summary: Stages and commits only the manifest files that actually changed in a repo, using ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT to bypass the protected-branch guard for automated syncs, and safely unstages on commit failure.

nodes.name: _push_if_protected
nodes.summary: Pushes the just-committed branch when it is main/master and has a configured upstream, keeping local main from diverging from origin after an auto-commit.

nodes.name: _post_gh_pr_create_repo_has_board
nodes.summary: Checks via a GraphQL query whether the current repo has at least one projectV2 board, so the retry/poll budget for board-status sync is skipped entirely on boardless repos.

nodes.name: _normalize_file
nodes.summary: Rewrites one manifest JSON file in place with a given jq renorm program, backing it up once and touching it only when the normalized content actually differs from the original.

nodes.name: marketplaces.json
nodes.summary: Public (github-sourced) Claude Code plugin marketplace manifest mapping short marketplace names to their `owner/repo` source, maintained as ground truth for reconcile.sh and restore.sh.

nodes.name: plugins.json
nodes.summary: Manifest of installed Claude Code plugins in `<plugin>@<marketplace>` form for public/github-sourced marketplaces, used by restore.sh to reinstall a fresh PC's plugin set.

nodes.name: publish-sync.sh
nodes.summary: Publishes locally-committed claude/plugin manifest changes (that plugin-sync.sh could never push directly due to branch protection) by building a commit with git plumbing against origin/main, opening/merging a PR, then fast-forwarding or rebasing local main onto the published result.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds commits via hash-object/read-tree/write-tree/commit-tree plumbing against a scratch index rather than checking out a branch, to avoid disturbing the user's working tree.

nodes.name: reconcile.sh
nodes.summary: Full-recompute drift detector/repair for the dotfiles plugin manifests: treats the shared ~/.claude-shared/plugins SSOT as ground truth and rebuilds claude/plugin/{marketplaces,plugins}.json to match exactly, in `--check` (diff report) or `--apply` (rewrite + commit) mode.

nodes.name: restore.sh
nodes.summary: Reinstalls Claude Code plugins and marketplaces from the dotfiles manifest onto a fresh or existing PC, resolving the account-routed $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR and supporting add-only (default) or `--sync` (two-way, prunes local surplus) modes.

nodes.name: _repo_target
nodes.summary: Parses a repo's origin remote URL (https or SSH, github.com or any GHES host) into a `host/owner/repo` string for `gh --repo` targeting.

nodes.name: _fetch_origin
nodes.summary: Fetches the latest origin/main ref for a repo before the publish commit is built against it.

nodes.name: _build_publish_commit
nodes.summary: Builds the publish commit against origin/main using git plumbing (scratch index, write-tree, commit-tree) instead of checking out a branch or touching the real working tree.

nodes.name: _publish_branch
nodes.summary: Pushes the constructed publish commit to a new remote branch as the basis for the manifest-sync PR.

nodes.name: _open_and_merge_pr
nodes.summary: Opens a pull request for the manifest-sync branch via `gh pr create` and merges it, since both plugin repos require changes to land through a PR rather than a direct push.

nodes.name: _publish_manifest_diff
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestration for publishing one repo's manifest diff: fetches origin, builds and pushes the publish commit, and drives the PR open/merge flow.

nodes.name: _ahead_commits_are_pure_sync
nodes.summary: Checks whether every local commit main is ahead of origin/main by is a pure `chore(claude-plugin): sync manifest` commit, gating whether local main can be safely rebased/cleaned up.

nodes.name: _collapse_stuck_sync_commits
nodes.summary: Rebases local main past redundant pure-sync commits once their content has landed on origin/main via the merged PR, using git's patch-id detection to avoid losing unlanded sync commits.

nodes.name: _cleanup_local_main_if_pure_sync
nodes.summary: Fast-forwards or rebases local main onto the published origin/main, but only when every ahead commit is a pure sync commit and the working tree is clean.

nodes.name: _public_publish_allowed
nodes.summary: Gates whether a manifest diff is allowed to be published to the public repo, guarding against leaking internal/private marketplace entries.

nodes.name: _usage
nodes.summary: Prints reconcile.sh's usage text describing the `--check` (default) and `--apply` modes.

nodes.name: _read_json_or
nodes.summary: Reads and compacts a JSON file, falling back to a default value when the file is missing, empty, or invalid JSON.

nodes.name: _diff_marketplaces
nodes.summary: Computes the add/remove/change diff between the SSOT marketplace set and the local manifest.

nodes.name: _diff_plugins
nodes.summary: Computes the add/remove diff between the SSOT plugin set and the local manifest.

nodes.name: _run_check
nodes.summary: Runs `--check` mode: prints the SSOT-vs-manifest diff for public and (on internal PCs) private manifests and exits non-zero when drift is found.

nodes.name: _commit_if_changed
nodes.summary: Stages and commits the reconciled manifest files in a repo only if they actually changed.

nodes.name: _run_apply
nodes.summary: Runs `--apply` mode: rewrites the public (and private) manifest files to exactly match the SSOT and commits the change.

nodes.name: _usage
nodes.summary: Prints restore.sh's usage text describing --user/--all-accounts, --sync, and --dry-run flags.

nodes.name: _target_config_dirs
nodes.summary: Resolves the account-routed $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR value(s) to restore into, so plugin installs land in the config directory the interactive `claude` wrapper actually uses.

nodes.name: _restore_from
nodes.summary: Adds/installs marketplaces and plugins from a manifest directory into a target config dir via `claude plugin` commands.

nodes.name: _local_plugins
nodes.summary: Lists the plugins currently installed locally in a target config dir, used by `--sync` mode to compute what should be pruned.

nodes.name: _prune_pass
nodes.summary: In `--sync` mode, uninstalls/removes local marketplaces and plugins that are absent from the SSOT manifest, making the local set exactly match it.

nodes.name: _run_for_config_dir
nodes.summary: Top-level per-account orchestration that runs the restore (and optional prune) pass for one resolved config directory.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index and charter for the `.ssot` policy directory: explains the one-policy-per-file principle, that AGENTS.md files are thin routers into it, and lists every policy file with a one-line description of its scope.

nodes.name: command-delivery-model.md
nodes.summary: SSOT policy deciding whether a new command should be a shell function or a PATH executable, with a decision tree covering interactive vs non-interactive use and reference implementations of both forms.

nodes.name: command-design-pattern.md
nodes.summary: SSOT for shell command structure covering naming rules, Type 1 (simple function), Type 2A (positional dispatcher, e.g. `gwt`), Type 2B (flag-triggered dispatcher, e.g. `gb`), passthrough rules, help integration, deprecated-alias strategy, SOLID criteria, and test requirements.

nodes.name: command-guidelines.md
nodes.summary: SSOT for command/help interface UX: standard help entry points, output policy, hierarchical output rules, section-data SSOT pattern, and the naming convention for help functions.

nodes.name: commit-message-standard.md
nodes.summary: SSOT for git branch naming and commit message format, including the semantic commit template, type/category/audience classification, a daily 'Jira First' routine, and automated work_log.txt recording.

nodes.name: discussions-policy.md
nodes.summary: SSOT for GitHub Discussions operation: category matrix, routing decision tree, Discussion<->Issue conversion rules, and how the Lessons category feeds docs/guide/learnings/.

nodes.name: env-vars.md
nodes.summary: Catalog of cross-skill/cross-tool environment variables (e.g. GH_DISABLE_AI_METRICS, GH_PR_MERGE_SKIP_BOARD_CHECK, GH_PROJECT_STATUS_SYNC) with usage notes and instructions for adding a new entry.

nodes.name: github-project-board.md
nodes.summary: SSOT for the GitHub Project v2 kanban board workflow: board structure, status field options, card lifecycle by type, auto-transition rules, closing-keyword policy, and initial board setup.

nodes.name: local-test-policy.md
nodes.summary: SSOT policy that CI runs lint-only while full tests (`mise run test`) run locally via the pre-push git hook, with the mechanism, per-environment behavior, opt-out policy, and rollback triggers.

nodes.name: pc-environment.md
nodes.summary: SSOT inventory of the 5 PCs this dotfiles setup runs on, the `~/.dotfiles-setup-mode` mode switch (public/internal/external), and which behaviors (account routing, git host resolution) change per mode.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Charter and move-log for docs/archive/: explains what belongs here (retired decision analyses, postmortems, expired todos, internally-sensitive material), documents the directory structure with move reasons, and accumulates a dated log of each archival move.

nodes.name: jira-workflow-decision.md
nodes.summary: Archived decision analysis comparing a 'Jira First' vs 'Code First' workflow approach, including a company-context assessment, phased rollout plan, exception cases, and the final recommendation and decision.

nodes.name: todo-defer-2026-05.md
nodes.summary: Archived Phase 2 refactoring review plan (plugin loader `load_category` abstraction and UX library interface split) with review criteria, triggers, a pre-implementation checklist, and a decision record — deferred past its 2026-05 due date.

nodes.name: todo-ollama.md
nodes.summary: Archived todo list tracking Ollama WSL+Docker hybrid integration work (completed phases, in-progress GLM-4.7-Flash validation, and next steps), with most items completed and residual items kept for reference.

nodes.name: workflow-git-jira-confluence-automation-guide-validated.md
nodes.summary: Large validated guide for a Git/Jira/Confluence 'Air-Gap Bridge' automation strategy: daily routine, commit-message standardization, make-jira/make-confluence skill proposals, SOLID/risk analysis, copy-paste templates, and peer-review integration notes across multiple reviewers.

nodes.name: AIE_member_code_mapping.md
nodes.summary: Confidential part-lead-only mapping table linking JIRA Story summary letter codes (Greek letters like delta, xi, lambda) to actual team members, plus the summary-prefix format rules and JQL query examples used across the AI Enable part's evaluation JIRA structure.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Greek letters as permanent pseudonymous member codes to keep JIRA summaries anonymized while remaining stable identifiers across history.

nodes.name: JIRA_Story_draft_2026-C.md
nodes.summary: One AI-assisted draft (variant C) of a JIRA Story structure for the 2026 AI Enable part evaluation cycle, covering both part-level and individual-level stories across four weighted evaluation categories.

nodes.name: JIRA_Story_draft_2026-CX.md
nodes.summary: A second AI-assisted draft (variant CX) proposing a JIRA Story skeleton for the same 2026 AI Enable part and individual evaluation structure, written in a more conversational, unpolished tone as a comparison alternative.
