nodes.name: commit-msg-rules.sh
nodes.summary: SSOT config defining Conventional Commits validation rules for the commit-msg hook: 8 allowed commit types, subject/body length limits, forbidden temporary-commit patterns, an optional JIRA-key regex, and warning message templates.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure variable-declaration shell file (arrays and regex strings) with no functions; consumed by sourcing rather than execution.

nodes.name: hook-config.sh
nodes.summary: SSOT config for the 2-tier git hook system: secret/private-key detection regex, conflict-marker patterns, debug-code patterns, forbidden filenames/paths, large-file threshold, and expected shebangs, sourced by both the global and project-level pre-commit hooks.

nodes.name: pre-push-rules.sh
nodes.summary: SSOT config for the pre-push hook: the protected-branch list blocking direct pushes, and an opt-in upstream leak guard whose regex patterns (UPSTREAM_REMOTES_ERE, LEAK_PATTERNS_ERE) default to empty/inert until a user exports values to scan for internal-pattern leaks before pushing to an identified upstream remote.

nodes.name: prepare-commit-msg-rules.sh
nodes.summary: SSOT config for the prepare-commit-msg hook: JIRA-key extraction pattern, branch-name glob patterns that trigger templating, and commit message templates (with/without JIRA key) used to auto-generate draft commit messages from branch names.

nodes.name: ANTI_PATTERNS.md
nodes.summary: SSOT catalog of git-hook anti-patterns (subshell sourcing that loses functions/aliases, alias/function name conflicts, unnecessary wrapper functions, tools/custom auto-sourcing regressions, library-purity violations) with paired bad/good code examples.

nodes.name: HOOK_WORKFLOW.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide to the 2-tier git pre-commit hook workflow: full execution flow diagram, PHASE 1 user-level checks (secrets, conflict markers, whitespace, debug code, large files, email identity) and PHASE 2 project-level checks (shebang, function naming, UX library usage, anti-patterns), a worked example, test procedures, and a 10-question FAQ.

nodes.name: POST_COMMIT_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Setup guide for the post-commit hook covering work-log tracking and JIRA-key extraction, three installation options, environment-variable customization, a worked installation example against a real project (litellm), a troubleshooting FAQ, and a setup checklist.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index doc for the git/doc directory: summarizes POST_COMMIT_GUIDE.md and HOOK_WORKFLOW.md, gives quick-start commands and a frequently-asked-questions section, and links out to the related implementation and configuration files behind the hooks.

nodes.name: SSH_SETUP_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step guide to generating an SSH key pair, registering it with the ssh-agent and an Enterprise GitHub instance's web UI, testing the connection, and switching git remote URLs from HTTPS to SSH.

nodes.name: alias_conflict_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that flags a staged file defining both an alias and a function with the same (or dash/underscore-normalized) name, since that dual-definition causes zsh parse errors on reload.

nodes.name: check_alias_function_conflict
nodes.summary: Extracts alias names and function names from a file, compares them (with dash/underscore normalization), and reports a blocking conflict unless the alias intentionally points to the function.

nodes.name: alias_location_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check enforcing that newly-added aliases live under shell-common/aliases/ and functions live under shell-common/functions/, refusing to flag files already tracked in git.

nodes.name: check_alias_location
nodes.summary: Given a repo-relative file path, checks the two placement rules (no aliases in functions/, no functions in aliases/) and appends a diagnostic to the output file when a new file violates one.

nodes.name: auto_execution_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that scans sourced shell-common function/integration files for top-level code (echo, source, function calls, direct-exec guards) that would run unconditionally when the file is sourced during shell init.

nodes.name: check_auto_execution_in_sourced_files
nodes.summary: Writes the staged/worktree version of a sourced file to a temp file, runs an awk scan for top-level output/sourcing/exec-guard patterns outside function bodies, and reports a blocking violation for suspicious matches.

nodes.name: backup_file_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that scans the repository for accidentally committed or untracked '*.backup.*' files and reports them for cleanup.

nodes.name: check_backup_files
nodes.summary: Finds all '*.backup.*' files under the repo root (excluding .git), writes their repo-relative paths to the output file, and returns non-zero if any exist.

nodes.name: closing_keyword_check.sh
nodes.summary: Commit-msg hook check that rejects GitHub closing keywords other than 'Closes'/'Fixes' (e.g. Refs, Resolves, See, References), since those either don't auto-close issues or violate the AgentToolbox rollup policy.

nodes.name: check_closing_keyword
nodes.summary: Scans a commit message file for forbidden closing-keyword patterns and prints all matches plus remediation guidance to stderr when found.

nodes.name: custom_tools_entrypoint_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check ensuring tools/custom scripts that define main() and call it near EOF also carry a direct-exec guard, preventing accidental execution if the file is ever sourced.

nodes.name: check_auto_executable_in_custom
nodes.summary: For shell-common/tools/custom/*.sh files, stages the content, looks for a trailing unguarded 'main' call near EOF, and reports a blocking violation if no BASH_SOURCE-based guard is present.

nodes.name: direct_exec_guard_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check requiring every shell-common/tools/custom/*.sh script to contain a recognized direct-exec guard pattern so it cannot auto-execute if accidentally sourced.

nodes.name: check_direct_exec_guard
nodes.summary: Stages a tools/custom file's content and checks it against known BASH_SOURCE-based guard regexes, reporting a blocking violation with fix guidance when none match.

nodes.name: gh_api_type_check.sh
nodes.summary: Heuristic warning-only check for 'gh api graphql' -f/-F flag/type mismatches (String vs Int GraphQL variables) and missing '# Variables:' annotations, guarding against the silent 422 failures from issue #395/#384.

nodes.name: check_gh_api_type_mapping
nodes.summary: Scans a staged file for '-F' with literal non-numeric values, '-f' with literal numeric values, and 'gh api graphql' blocks missing a preceding 'Variables:' annotation, writing WARN entries for each signal found.

nodes.name: hardcoded_home_path_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that blocks staged shell/bash/zsh config diffs adding hardcoded '/home/<user>' or '/Users/<user>' paths, guarding the multi-PC portability regression from issue #737.

nodes.name: check_hardcoded_home_path
nodes.summary: Scans only the added lines of a staged diff for hardcoded absolute home-directory paths (excluding docs/tests/comments and an opt-out marker) and reports a blocking violation with matched lines.

nodes.name: help_integrity_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check requiring any function ending in 'help' to be registered in HELP_DESCRIPTIONS (locally or in the global my_help.sh registry) or renamed with a leading underscore if internal.

nodes.name: check_help_integrity
nodes.summary: Finds functions named '*help' in a file and verifies each is registered in HELP_DESCRIPTIONS locally or in shell-common/functions/my_help.sh (with dash/underscore normalization), reporting unregistered ones as violations.

nodes.name: library_purity_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check enforcing that shell-common/functions and integrations files stay side-effect-free at file scope: no top-level main/_main calls, interactive read/select prompts, or package-manager install commands outside a function.

nodes.name: check_library_purity
nodes.summary: Runs an awk scan over a staged shell-common file tracking function-body depth, flags top-level main-call/interactive-prompt/install-command lines (while allowing them inside aliases or one-line functions), and reports blocking violations.

nodes.name: main_branch_guard.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that blocks direct commits on the main/master branch, with an ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT=1 escape hatch for intentional exceptions.

nodes.name: check_main_branch_guard
nodes.summary: Reads the current branch via 'git symbolic-ref', honors the ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT escape hatch and detached-HEAD case, and returns non-zero (exporting the branch name) when the branch is main or master.

nodes.name: naming_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check enforcing snake_case function names (flagging dash-form definitions) and detecting likely inconsistent naming between a file's own filename and the function names referenced inside it.

nodes.name: check_function_naming
nodes.summary: Greps a file for dash-form function definitions and reports each with the snake_case-corrected name as a naming violation.

nodes.name: check_naming_violations
nodes.summary: For files whose name contains an underscore, cross-references defined function names (excluding short names and ux_lib calls) against string literals in the file to catch stale/renamed references.

nodes.name: pipe_loop_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check detecting the 'done <<EOF | cmd' anti-pattern where piping after a while/for loop's heredoc redirection creates a subshell that breaks variable propagation and 'set -x' tracing.

nodes.name: check_pipe_loop_antipattern
nodes.summary: Greps a file for 'done <<...|' and 'done <...|' patterns and reports each match with a fix suggestion referencing the anti-patterns doc.

nodes.name: postmortem_init_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check specific to bash/main.bash and zsh/main.zsh that detects re-introduced auto-sourcing of shell-common/tools/custom utilities during shell init, guarding a known postmortem regression.

nodes.name: check_init_auto_sourcing
nodes.summary: Stages an init file's content and runs an awk scan (including inside 'for ... in tools/custom' loops) for source/dot/safe_source calls targeting tools/custom, reporting a blocking violation on any match.

nodes.name: shared.sh
nodes.summary: Common helper for git/hooks/pre-commit's check scripts, providing write_staged_or_worktree_to_tmp to materialize a file's staged (or worktree-fallback) content into a temp path for scanning.
nodes.languageNotes: Prefers the git-staged blob (':path' via git show) over the worktree copy so checks scan exactly what will be committed.

nodes.name: write_staged_or_worktree_to_tmp
nodes.summary: Writes the staged git-index content of a file to a temp path if it exists there, otherwise falls back to copying the worktree file; returns failure if neither is available.

nodes.name: shebang_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check verifying each shell script's first line matches the shebang expected for its directory (shell-common/tools/custom, shell-common, bash/, zsh/), with per-directory overrides via env vars.

nodes.name: get_expected_shebang
nodes.summary: Determines the expected shebang line for a given absolute path based on which directory tree it falls under, honoring DOTFILES_HOOKS_SHEBANG_* environment overrides.

nodes.name: check_shebang_violation
nodes.summary: Compares a file's actual first line against its expected shebang (via get_expected_shebang) and returns non-zero on mismatch.

nodes.name: shellcheck_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check running ShellCheck static analysis over staged shell/bash/zsh files, using stricter '-S info -x' rules for shell-common (sourceable, sh-portable) and default '-S warning' elsewhere.

nodes.name: check_shellcheck
nodes.summary: Iterates over the staged file list, runs shellcheck with directory-appropriate strictness on each shell script, appends violations to a file, and reports a truncated summary plus a total count.

nodes.name: subshell_sourcing_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check detecting the anti-pattern of capturing a sourced script's output into a variable ('var=$(. file.sh)'), which breaks function propagation into the caller's shell.

nodes.name: check_subshell_sourcing
nodes.summary: Greps a file for 'var=$( ... .sh)' style command-substitution sourcing and reports each match with a fix suggestion to use direct '. file' sourcing instead.

nodes.name: ux_usage_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that, for files already using ux_lib functions, flags stray raw 'echo' calls that should instead go through ux_lib output helpers per the repo's UX conventions.

nodes.name: check_ux_library_usage
nodes.summary: Skips files that don't use ux_lib or that guard ux_ calls with a command-existence check, otherwise counts non-empty raw 'echo' lines (excluding pipelines/substitutions) as violations.

nodes.name: check_port_registry.sh
nodes.summary: Validates PORTS.md's port registry table, checking that each row's index is unique and its backend/frontend/db ports follow the decade-block formula (backend = 9200 + index*10).

nodes.name: debug_pip_config.sh
nodes.summary: Diagnostic script that reports pip's environment variables, config file locations/contents, symlink status, proxy settings, and Python/pip/pyenv versions for troubleshooting pip configuration issues.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints the script's usage/help text describing what the pip diagnostic report covers.

nodes.name: disable-git-crypt-local.sh
nodes.summary: Neutralizes leftover local git-crypt smudge/clean/required config on the dotfiles repo so historical git-crypt filters can't corrupt checkouts (host-side safety net for issue #594); idempotent and always exits 0.

nodes.name: flush-transfer.sh
nodes.summary: One-shot cleanup script that removes all tracked files under transfer/, commits the removal, and pushes — used to erase a one-time PC-to-PC transfer payload after it has been consumed.

nodes.name: lint_docs_filenames.sh
nodes.summary: Lints docs/ Markdown filenames for kebab-case naming, exempting conventional uppercase files (README/AGENTS/GEMINI/CLAUDE) and enforcing failure only within designated tiers (docs/adr, docs/requirement) while warning elsewhere.

nodes.name: is_enforced
nodes.summary: Checks whether a doc path falls under the enforced directories (docs/adr, docs/requirement) where kebab-case violations fail the build rather than just warn.

nodes.name: setup-skills-ssot.sh
nodes.summary: Links claude/skills/ as the single source of truth for Claude Code skills into OpenCode, Gemini, and Codex config directories via entry-level symlink composition, migrating legacy directory-level symlinks and honoring an optional Codex allowlist to stay under Codex's description-length budget.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses POSIX-ish bash with here-string iteration and readlink -f normalization instead of associative arrays to detect and migrate legacy vs. SSOT-owned symlinks.

nodes.name: read_codex_allowlist
nodes.summary: Reads and normalizes the optional .codex-allowlist file, stripping comments and blank lines to produce a plain skill-name list.

nodes.name: codex_skill_is_allowed
nodes.summary: Tests whether a given skill name passes the Codex allowlist gate — an empty allowlist means every skill is allowed.

nodes.name: collect_codex_homes
nodes.summary: Enumerates and de-duplicates candidate Codex home directories (CODEX_HOME, ~/.codex, XDG config, ~/.cod) that actually exist on disk.

nodes.name: link_skills_compose
nodes.summary: Builds or migrates an entry-level composition directory for a tool (OpenCode/Gemini) where each SSOT skill is symlinked in as a child entry, preserving user-added entries and warning on foreign symlinks.

nodes.name: link_skills_individual
nodes.summary: Creates individual per-skill symlinks from the SSOT into a target directory for tools that don't use entry-level composition.

nodes.name: link_skills_individual_codex
nodes.summary: Codex-specific symlink linker that preserves the local .system directory, applies the optional allowlist, and prunes stale/removed skill links while reporting linked/migrated/skipped/pruned counts.

nodes.name: verify_link
nodes.summary: Post-setup verification that reports the symlink/entry counts and .system presence for each linked skills directory.

nodes.name: verify-config-files.sh
nodes.summary: Post-setup.sh integrity check that parses each deployed config symlink target as JSON (where applicable) and scans for invisible corruption bytes (BOM/NBSP/NUL), guarding against a known git-crypt smudge corruption pattern (#594).

nodes.name: _scan_bytes
nodes.summary: Detects BOM, NBSP (U+00A0), and NUL bytes in a config file, reporting the corrupting byte's location.

nodes.name: _parse_json
nodes.summary: Validates a file as well-formed JSON using python3 or jq, whichever is available, skipping the check gracefully if neither is installed.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Overview document for the scripts/maintenance/ toolkit, describing the shebang analyzer, bash regression checkers (shell and Python variants), and Codex skill budget checker, along with usage scenarios for each.

nodes.name: analyze_shebang_report.sh
nodes.summary: Comprehensive shebang-consistency analyzer that scans .sh/.bash files for bash-specific feature usage (arrays, [[ ]], parameter expansion, etc.), classifies each file as posix-ok/bash-required/wrong-shebang/missing-shebang, and prints a colorized findings report with prioritized fix recommendations.

nodes.name: detect_bash_features
nodes.summary: Scans a file against a table of bash-specific regex patterns (arrays, [[ ]], BASH_SOURCE, parameter expansion, etc.) and returns the names of features found.

nodes.name: get_shebang
nodes.summary: Extracts and normalizes a file's shebang line for comparison against its detected feature requirements.

nodes.name: analyze_file
nodes.summary: Core per-file analysis routine that combines shebang inspection and bash-feature detection to classify a file into one of the report categories and update running statistics.

nodes.name: print_stats
nodes.summary: Prints the summary statistics table (counts per classification category) for the analyzed file set.

nodes.name: print_priority_files
nodes.summary: Prints the highest-priority files needing a shebang fix, ranked by severity.

nodes.name: print_findings
nodes.summary: Prints the detailed per-category findings section of the shebang analysis report.

nodes.name: print_source_only
nodes.summary: Prints the list of files that are source-only (not directly executable) along with their shebang status.

nodes.name: print_bash_required
nodes.summary: Prints the list of files detected as requiring bash-specific features.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full scan — walks the target files, calls analyze_file on each, then renders all report sections in order.

nodes.name: check_bash_status.sh
nodes.summary: Bash regression test harness that syntax-checks and sources every .bash file under a target directory, either in isolated fresh shells or as one chained shell, capturing bash -x trace logs for each run.

nodes.name: show_help
nodes.summary: Prints CLI usage for the ROOT_DIR/--mode/--logdir options and example invocations.

nodes.name: run_isolated
nodes.summary: Runs each target .bash file in its own fresh shell subprocess, capturing a trace log and pass/fail result per file.

nodes.name: run_chain
nodes.summary: Sources all target .bash files sequentially within a single shell to catch cross-file interaction regressions.

nodes.name: fix_crlf_issue.sh
nodes.summary: One-shot repair script for corporate PC environments that sets git core.autocrlf=false, converts CRLF line endings to LF across shell scripts, and ensures ~/.config exists with correct permissions.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Operational guide for the shell-common/ module covering lint/format/reload commands, POSIX-compatibility and cross-shell-sourcing golden rules, a file-placement decision tree, and the 3-step pattern for adding a new tool integration.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reference for the shell-common/ directory — its structure (env/aliases/functions/tools/projects), module loading order, environment/alias/function module catalogs, instructions for adding new shared modules, and configuration file templates.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Environment-specific setup script that symlinks tool configuration files (pip, uv, cargo, nuget, npm, bun, opencode) into their canonical locations, applies security cert configuration, resolves per-account settings (e.g. Knox ID for opencode), and verifies the resulting config after installation.
nodes.languageNotes: POSIX sh throughout (no bash arrays); uses prefixed sh-compatible variable names in place of associative arrays for the SSOT config values.

nodes.name: _prepare_config_target
nodes.summary: Prepares a config target path for symlink creation, removing an existing symlink or backing up an existing regular file/directory before it's replaced.

nodes.name: _restore_config_from_backup
nodes.summary: Restores a previously backed-up config file if a setup step needs to roll back a target it just prepared.

nodes.name: cleanup_local_files
nodes.summary: Removes stale local override files that are no longer needed after setup.

nodes.name: copy_local_files
nodes.summary: Copies local (non-symlinked, machine-specific) config file templates into place, creating them from .example templates when missing.

nodes.name: _pcae_write_email_export
nodes.summary: Writes the resolved Claude account email(s) to an export file consumed by other setup steps.

nodes.name: _prompt_claude_account_emails
nodes.summary: Interactively prompts the user for Claude account email addresses when they aren't already configured.

nodes.name: setup_security_config
nodes.summary: Symlinks the appropriate CA certificate bundle (external vs internal) based on the detected network environment.

nodes.name: setup_npm_symlink
nodes.summary: Symlinks the tracked .npmrc into place for npm configuration.

nodes.name: setup_bun_config
nodes.summary: Symlinks the tracked bunfig.toml into place for Bun configuration.

nodes.name: _resolve_knox_id
nodes.summary: Resolves the current machine/account's Knox ID, used to select the correct OpenCode config variant.

nodes.name: setup_opencode_config
nodes.summary: Symlinks and configures the OpenCode config file, selecting the variant appropriate to the resolved Knox ID.

nodes.name: verify_config
nodes.summary: Verifies that a symlinked config file resolves to an existing, readable target after setup.

nodes.name: setup_local_files
nodes.summary: Orchestrates creation of all local (non-tracked) config files from their .example templates.

nodes.name: setup_uv_config
nodes.summary: Symlinks the tracked uv.toml into place for the uv Python package manager.

nodes.name: setup_pip_config
nodes.summary: Symlinks the tracked pip.conf into place for pip configuration.

nodes.name: setup_cargo_config
nodes.summary: Symlinks the tracked Cargo config.toml into place for Rust/Cargo.

nodes.name: setup_nuget_config
nodes.summary: Symlinks the tracked NuGet.Config into place for .NET package management.

nodes.name: setup_rpm_repo
nodes.summary: Configures RPM package repository definitions for the detected Linux distribution.

nodes.name: setup_apt_sources
nodes.summary: Configures APT package source lists/keys for Debian-based systems.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that runs the full setup sequence — local files, security config, tool configs, package repos — and reports verification results.

nodes.name: claude_plugins.sh
nodes.summary: Alias shortcuts for Claude Code marketplace plugin management commands — opening, listing, syncing, and generating documentation for plugins.

nodes.name: core.sh
nodes.summary: Core POSIX aliases for everyday shell use (listing, navigation, file-op confirmations, colored grep) plus the src() function that force-reloads the shell from the canonical dotfiles tree, bypassing any worktree-leaked DOTFILES_ROOT.

nodes.name: src
nodes.summary: Reloads the current shell (bash or zsh) from the canonical dotfiles checkout, resetting DOTFILES_ROOT/SHELL_COMMON so a worktree-inherited root can't leak into the reload (issue #310), then runs an optional one-line WSL health check.

nodes.name: directory.sh
nodes.summary: Directory-navigation aliases and functions for the dotfiles root, Windows/WSL mount paths, and the PARA (archive/area/project/resource) organizational structure, plus shortcuts to the symlink-manager tool.

nodes.name: disk_usage.sh
nodes.summary: Small set of du/sort aliases for summarizing disk usage of the current directory, its subdirectories, and SQL dump files.

nodes.name: git.sh
nodes.summary: Portable git aliases (status, add, commit, push/pull variants, log, branch, diff, remote, cherry-pick) plus the hook_check() function that dispatches to the git-hooks diagnostic script.

nodes.name: hook_check
nodes.summary: Locates and invokes shell-common/tools/custom/hook_check.sh to diagnose the repo's git hook configuration, resolving DOTFILES_ROOT dynamically if it isn't already set.

nodes.name: help_system_aliases.sh
nodes.summary: Dash-case aliases for the category help system and the full set of Ollama LLM management commands (install, serve, pull, run, status, restart).

nodes.name: kill.sh
nodes.summary: Port-management helpers — kill_by_port() force-kills whatever process holds a given TCP port, and check_port() reports what's listening on it, exposed via the kp/lp aliases.

nodes.name: kill_by_port
nodes.summary: Looks up the PID(s) bound to a given port via lsof and force-kills them, warning if none are found.

nodes.name: mytool.sh
nodes.summary: Aliases for the custom mytool suite — help text, source-bundling (srcpack), hardware-info reporting, AGENTS.md scaffolding, and the dotfiles test-suite runner.

nodes.name: obsidian_claude.sh
nodes.summary: Dash-form aliases exposing the obsidian_claude() and obsidian_clone() launcher functions defined in shell-common/functions/.

nodes.name: project-cli-aliases.sh
nodes.summary: Generic runner and per-project CLI wrappers (currently jiravis) that invoke a project's backend Python module via PYTHONPATH, plus a dedicated jiravis pytest test-runner with subcommands for full/func/api/coverage/parallel test runs.

nodes.name: _run_project_cli
nodes.summary: Generic helper that cd's into a PARA project directory, validates its backend Python module entry point exists, and execs it via `python -m` with PYTHONPATH set.

nodes.name: run_jiravis_test
nodes.summary: Dispatches jiravis pytest runs by subcommand (all/func/api/coverage/parallel), forwarding extra arguments to pytest.

nodes.name: _jiravis_test_help
nodes.summary: Prints the jira-test command's usage help, listing subcommands and example invocations.

nodes.name: system.sh
nodes.summary: System-administration aliases for process inspection, networking (ports, IP), monitoring (top/meminfo/cpuinfo), APT package management, and live log tailing.

nodes.name: work-aliases.sh
nodes.summary: Aliases wiring the work-log, make-jira, and make-confluence custom tools for logging work activity and generating Jira/Confluence reports from those logs.

nodes.name: aws.sh
nodes.summary: Loads AWS Bedrock shell environment variables by sourcing the gitignored aws/aws.local.sh when present; silently no-ops on external/public machines that talk directly to Anthropic.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the interactive-guard + local-override sourcing pattern shared by every file in shell-common/env/.

nodes.name: claude.sh
nodes.summary: Sets Claude Code environment variables (doc generator tool, skills path) and derives multi-account defaults (CLAUDE_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT, CLAUDE_ENABLED_ACCOUNTS) from the PC's internal/external setup mode, then loads a local override file.

nodes.name: development.sh
nodes.summary: Sets development-related environment variables for Python (PYTHONPATH, PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE), Node.js (NODE_ENV), and Java (JAVA_HOME, PATH), then loads a machine-specific override file.

nodes.name: dotenv.sh
nodes.summary: Sources the repo-root .env file at shell startup, first detecting whether it is still git-crypt encrypted (via strings/od/file fallbacks) to avoid sourcing binary data and breaking the shell.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-tier detection fallback (strings -> od -> file) keeps the encrypted-file check portable across minimal POSIX environments.

nodes.name: editor.sh
nodes.summary: Sets the default EDITOR, VISUAL, and GIT_EDITOR environment variables to vim.

nodes.name: fcitx.sh
nodes.summary: Opt-in (ENABLE_FCITX=true) Fcitx input method configuration for Korean input, exporting the required IM environment variables and auto-starting fcitx when a display session is present.

nodes.name: locale.sh
nodes.summary: Sets locale and terminal environment variables (LANG, LC_ALL en_US.UTF-8, TERM xterm-256color, TZ Asia/Seoul) for consistent encoding and terminal compatibility.

nodes.name: ollama.sh
nodes.summary: Sets default Ollama runtime environment variables (context length, GPU auto-detect, keep-alive) and dotfiles-specific backend selection (auto/local/docker) with docker container name, overridable before sourcing.

nodes.name: path.sh
nodes.summary: Builds up PATH with local bin, Go, dotfiles bash scripts, Snap, and Bun directories, and defines the clean_paths function that deduplicates PATH entries using shell-specific (bash/zsh) or POSIX-fallback logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements three separate dedup strategies (bash associative array, zsh associative array, POSIX positional-parameter loop) to stay portable across shells.

nodes.name: proxy.sh
nodes.summary: Sets default no-proxy exceptions for local/private networks (public/home environment defaults) and loads an environment-specific proxy.local.sh override for corporate/VPN proxy settings.

nodes.name: python_venv_prompt.sh
nodes.summary: Disables PS1 prompt mutation by virtualenv and pyenv-virtualenv so the shell's custom prompt styling stays consistent when a Python virtual environment is activated.

nodes.name: security.sh
nodes.summary: Configures SSH_AUTH_SOCK (XDG runtime dir or systemd fallback) and GPG_TTY when a TTY is available, then loads environment-specific CA certificate settings from security.local.sh.

nodes.name: _is_gitcrypt_encrypted
nodes.summary: Detects whether a file is still git-crypt encrypted by checking for the GITCRYPT magic header, trying strings, then od, then file as successive fallbacks.

nodes.name: clean_paths
nodes.summary: Deduplicates and normalizes PATH entries (dropping non-existent directories and trailing slashes), using bash associative arrays, zsh associative arrays, or a POSIX positional-parameter loop depending on the running shell.

nodes.name: claude_plugins_core.sh
nodes.summary: Core Claude Code plugin management functions (split from claude_plugins.sh): initializing the docs/marketplaces mirror tree, listing installed marketplaces/agents/commands/skills, syncing the plugin tree into docs/, and viewing a single plugin file's description.

nodes.name: init_plugins_docs
nodes.summary: Initializes the ~/.claude/docs/marketplaces mirror directory tree used to hold generated per-plugin documentation.

nodes.name: list_plugins
nodes.summary: Lists installed marketplaces, agents, commands, and skills by reading the plugins SSOT JSON files.

nodes.name: sync_plugins_structure
nodes.summary: Mirrors the live plugin directory tree into docs/ so plugin documentation can be edited in place.

nodes.name: view_plugin_info
nodes.summary: Extracts and prints the description metadata for a single plugin file, given its path.

nodes.name: _extract_yaml_field_fallback
nodes.summary: Manual line-scanning fallback that extracts a single YAML field from a plugin.json/marketplace.json-style file when a stricter parse fails.

nodes.name: _get_plugin_description
nodes.summary: Resolves a plugin's human-readable description, trying the YAML field extractor before falling back to a generic placeholder.

nodes.name: claude_plugins_docs_ko.sh
nodes.summary: Korean-language counterpart to the plugin docs generator: builds an AI prompt to draft per-plugin Korean documentation (`generate_plugin_doc_ko`), walks a marketplace's plugin directories recursively (`process_plugin_directory_ko`), and scaffolds the docs/marketplaces Korean directory structure (`create_plugin_structure_ko`).

nodes.name: _generate_plugin_doc_ko_prompt
nodes.summary: Builds the AI prompt text used to generate a Korean-language plugin README from a plugin's metadata.

nodes.name: generate_plugin_doc_ko
nodes.summary: Invokes the configured AI CLI with the Korean doc-generation prompt for a given plugin and writes the resulting README.

nodes.name: _generate_plugin_directory_readme_ko
nodes.summary: Generates a Korean directory-level README summarizing all plugins under a marketplace path, pulling each plugin's description via claude_plugins_core.sh's `_get_plugin_description`.

nodes.name: process_plugin_directory_ko
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a marketplace's plugin directories, generating per-plugin and per-directory Korean docs, with a `--force` flag to overwrite existing files.

nodes.name: create_plugin_structure_ko
nodes.summary: Scaffolds the Korean-language docs/marketplaces directory structure, seeding it via process_plugin_directory_ko for each installed marketplace.

nodes.name: claude_plugins_help.sh
nodes.summary: Registers the `claude-plugins-help` topic command describing the claude-plugins command family (list, sync, view-plugin-info), example usage, AI-tool integration, workflow, and directory structure notes.

nodes.name: claude_plugins_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `claude-plugins-help`; dispatches to command reference, view examples, workflow, structure, or a named section.

nodes.name: claude_stop_hook_install.sh
nodes.summary: Auto-installs the gh-issue-flow Stop hook into claude/settings.json on every interactive shell startup, closing a gap where multi-account users whose live settings.json predates issue #383 silently miss the hook that setup.sh's one-time migration would otherwise add.

nodes.name: _claude_install_gh_issue_flow_stop_hook
nodes.summary: Checks whether the current Claude settings.json already has the gh-issue-flow Stop hook and, if not, idempotently merges it in via jq, staying silent on the hot path.

nodes.name: cli_help.sh
nodes.summary: Registers the `cli-help` topic command covering a grab-bag of CLI utilities: repo/URL helpers, finrx, dmc, and smt commands plus general tips.

nodes.name: cli_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `cli-help`; dispatches to repos, urls, finrx, dmc, smt, tips, `--all`, or a named section.

nodes.name: cp_wdown.sh
nodes.summary: Thin POSIX wrapper exposing the bash-only `cp_wdown` implementation (which lives in tools/custom/ and is not auto-sourced) as a regular function callable from either bash or zsh.

nodes.name: cp_wdown
nodes.summary: Builds the path to the bash-only tools/custom/cp_wdown.sh implementation and invokes it via bash, forwarding all arguments.

nodes.name: database_help.sh
nodes.summary: Registers three database CLI help-topic commands — `mysql-help`, `psql-help`, and `redis-help` — each covering the tool's service management and core command reference.

nodes.name: mysql_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `mysql-help`; dispatches to service management or the command reference section.

nodes.name: psql_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `psql-help`; dispatches to primary or low-level command sections.

nodes.name: redis_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `redis-help`; dispatches to commands or environment-variable sections.

nodes.name: devops_help.sh
nodes.summary: Registers three DevOps CLI help-topic commands — `docker-help` (including compose-file detection in the current directory and a use-case recommendation engine), `proxy-help`/`proxy-check`, and `dproxy-help` — for Docker, HTTP proxy, and Docker-proxy workflows respectively.

nodes.name: docker_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `docker-help`; dispatches to compose/basics/resources/utilities sections, a directory-aware recommendation, or the reverse command-name lookup.

nodes.name: _docker_help_recommend
nodes.summary: Inspects the current directory for compose files and running containers to recommend the most relevant docker/docker-compose commands for the detected setup.

nodes.name: _docker_help_catalog
nodes.summary: Static catalog mapping docker/docker-compose subcommands to their help-section slug, used by the reverse lookup.

nodes.name: _docker_help_reverse
nodes.summary: Given a docker subcommand name, looks up and prints the matching help section by normalizing aliases against the catalog.

nodes.name: _docker_help_raw
nodes.summary: Prints the raw docker-help catalog entries, optionally filtered, for scripting/piping consumption.

nodes.name: proxy_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `proxy-help`; dispatches to diagnostics, commands, set/unset, git-proxy, or related sections.

nodes.name: proxy_check
nodes.summary: Runs the proxy diagnostics: checks env vars, connectivity, and git proxy config, printing a pass/fail summary.

nodes.name: dproxy_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `dproxy-help`; dispatches to commands, config, or reference sections for the Docker-proxy helper.

nodes.name: devx.sh
nodes.summary: Implements `devx`, the Type 2A positional-dispatcher dev helper that routes lint/fix to `mise run <task>` and lint-helpfunc/lint-deadcode/stat to in-process checks, replacing tools/dev.sh and absorbing its full sub-command surface.

nodes.name: devx
nodes.summary: Type 2A dispatcher: routes `devx <subcommand>` to the matching internal helper (lint/fix/fmt/lint-helpfunc/lint-deadcode/stat) or to devx_help for `-h`/`--help`/empty input, erroring fast on unknown subcommands.

nodes.name: _devx_dotfiles_root
nodes.summary: Resolves the dotfiles repo root, honoring worktree layouts and the DOTFILES_ROOT_NO_CANONICALIZE override.

nodes.name: _devx_stat
nodes.summary: Computes and prints repo statistics (e.g. file/function counts) for the `devx stat` subcommand.

nodes.name: _devx_lint_helpfunc
nodes.summary: Scans shell-common/functions for help functions that don't follow the required naming/registration pattern and reports violations.
nodes.languageNotes: The bundled extraction script mis-measured this function's line range (reported 126-126) due to brace-matching limits on its shell fallback regex; the range here was corrected by direct source inspection.

nodes.name: _devx_lint_deadcode
nodes.summary: Scans internal (underscore-prefixed) functions across shell-common/functions for ones with no remaining call sites and reports them as potential dead code.
nodes.languageNotes: Line range corrected from direct source inspection; the extraction script reported a collapsed 196-196 range for the same brace-matching reason as _devx_lint_helpfunc.

nodes.name: devx_help.sh
nodes.summary: Registers the `devx-help` topic content for the `devx` dispatcher — covering lint, fix, lint-helpfunc, lint-deadcode, and stat subcommands — invoked directly by `devx()` rather than through its own dash-form alias (a deliberate §7.6.1 deviation).

nodes.name: devx_help
nodes.summary: Entry point invoked by devx() for `-h`/`--help`/no-args; dispatches to the lint/fix/lint-helpfunc/lint-deadcode/stat sections or `--all`.

nodes.name: NAMING_CONVENTION.md
nodes.summary: Documents the mandatory naming convention for shell-common/functions/: snake_case internal function names paired with dash-form user-facing aliases and help text, with a copy-paste template and verification checklist for new function files.

nodes.name: agy_help.sh
nodes.summary: Registers the `agy-help` topic command describing Antigravity CLI (agy) usage — basic commands, setup/install, and OAuth token/PATH-conflict tips — following the directory's dash-form help-topic pattern.

nodes.name: agy_help
nodes.summary: Public entry point for `agy-help`; dispatches to the summary view, `--list`, `--all`, or a named section using the file's row-builder helpers.

nodes.name: ai_setup.sh
nodes.summary: Implements `ai-setup`/`ai-teardown`, a one-command orchestrator that creates multiple git worktrees and tmux sessions with 3-pane windows for parallel AI-assisted development, plus the matching teardown.

nodes.name: ai_setup
nodes.summary: Creates a git worktree per requested task, wires up a tmux session with a 3-pane window (editor/agent/shell) for each, and reports the resulting session layout.

nodes.name: ai_teardown
nodes.summary: Tears down the tmux sessions and git worktrees created by `ai_setup`, cleaning up branches and session state.

nodes.name: ai_tools_help.sh
nodes.summary: Bundles help-topic commands for four AI CLI tools (claude, codex, litellm/llm, ollama) plus Claude skill-metadata extraction (`get_claude_skills`), each following the dash-form help-topic pattern.

nodes.name: claude_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `claude-help`, dispatching Claude Code CLI help sections (mcp, recommended settings, setup, sandbox, config, statusline, skills, plugin).

nodes.name: get_claude_skills
nodes.summary: Discovers installed Claude Code skills across plugin and project directories and extracts each skill's name/description metadata for display via `claude-skills`.

nodes.name: _extract_skill_metadata
nodes.summary: Parses a SKILL.md frontmatter block to pull the skill's name and description, falling back to the manual field extractor when the primary parse misses.

nodes.name: _extract_skill_field_fallback
nodes.summary: Manual line-scanning fallback that extracts a single YAML frontmatter field from a SKILL.md when a stricter parser fails, handling multi-line and quoted values.

nodes.name: codex_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `codex-help`, dispatching Codex CLI help sections (basic usage, setup, interactive mode, tips).

nodes.name: litellm_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `litellm-help`/`llm-help`, dispatching LiteLLM proxy help sections.

nodes.name: ollama_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `ollama-help`, dispatching Ollama help sections including auto-detected local/docker install guidance.

nodes.name: _ollama_help_auto
nodes.summary: Detects whether Ollama is installed locally or via Docker and routes to the matching install-guidance helper.

nodes.name: _ollama_help_local
nodes.summary: Prints native/local Ollama installation and model-pull instructions for the current OS.

nodes.name: _ollama_help_docker
nodes.summary: Prints Docker-based Ollama setup instructions including container run flags and volume mounts.

nodes.name: _ollama_help_status
nodes.summary: Reports whether the Ollama service/daemon is currently running and reachable, with next-step guidance.

nodes.name: ai_usage.sh
nodes.summary: Wraps AI CLI invocations (claude/codex/gemini) to record per-call token usage and cost into usage.jsonl, then aggregates a run's totals into a human-readable summary — used by gh-issue-flow's detached background workers to catch runaway quota burn.
nodes.languageNotes: Normalizes three different runner usage-JSON shapes (claude/codex/gemini) into one comparable schema before persisting to usage.jsonl.

nodes.name: _ai_usage_fmt_int
nodes.summary: Formats an integer with thousands separators using only sh/awk, independent of locale.

nodes.name: _ai_usage_is_transient
nodes.summary: Classifies a runner failure as a transient error (e.g. stream idle timeout, partial response) eligible for retry.

nodes.name: _ai_usage_run
nodes.summary: Invokes the requested AI runner (claude/codex/gemini), captures its usage/cost JSON output, retries on transient failures, and appends a normalized record to usage.jsonl.

nodes.name: _ai_usage_summary
nodes.summary: Reads a run's usage.jsonl and prints an aggregated totals block (tokens, cache hits, cost) across all invocations.

nodes.name: bat_help.sh
nodes.summary: Registers the `bat-help` topic command covering the `bat` file-viewer's basic usage, line/language selection, display and git-integration options, and configuration.

nodes.name: bat_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `bat-help`; dispatches to summary, `--list`, `--all`, or a named section.

nodes.name: category_help.sh
nodes.summary: Implements `category-help`, which lists the shared help-topic categories and routes users toward `my-help <topic>`, lazily sourcing `my_help.sh` and registering its default descriptions on first use.

nodes.name: category_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `category-help`; dispatches to the categories list, topics list, `--all`, or a section.

nodes.name: _category_help_ensure_loaded
nodes.summary: Lazily sources my_help.sh if not already loaded and registers default help descriptions exactly once, so category-help works even before my-help has been invoked.

nodes.name: cc_help.sh
nodes.summary: Registers the `cc-help` topic command for ccusage (Claude Code usage tracker) covering install and the ccd/ccs/ccb command aliases.

nodes.name: cc_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for `cc-help`; dispatches to install, commands, `--all`, or a named section.

nodes.name: claude_allow_clean.sh
nodes.summary: Implements `claude-allow-clean`, which prunes junk entries from Claude Code's `.claude/settings.local.json` permissions.allow list — one-time session tokens, structurally broken multi-line artifacts, and entries that should never be pre-approved such as `--no-verify`.

nodes.name: claude_allow_clean
nodes.summary: Locates the target settings.local.json, applies the jq allow-list filter (optionally as a dry run), and reports how many entries were removed.

nodes.name: _claude_allow_clean_find_file
nodes.summary: Resolves the settings.local.json path to operate on, from an explicit argument or the default Claude config locations.

nodes.name: _claude_allow_clean_write_filter
nodes.summary: Builds the jq filter expression that drops junk permission entries from the allow list.

nodes.name: claude_plugin_list.sh
nodes.summary: Implements `claude-plugin-list`, printing installed Claude Code plugins grouped by the marketplace that provided them, reading the installed_plugins.json/known_marketplaces.json SSOT under CLAUDE_SHARED_PLUGINS_DIR or CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/plugins.

nodes.name: claude_plugin_list
nodes.summary: Reads installed_plugins.json and known_marketplaces.json, groups plugins by marketplace via jq, and prints a human-readable summary.

nodes.name: _cpl_resolve_dir
nodes.summary: Resolves the plugins config directory to read from, honoring CLAUDE_SHARED_PLUGINS_DIR before falling back to CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/plugins.

nodes.name: devx_pr_review_all.sh
nodes.summary: Pure POSIX argument parser for the devx:pr-review-all skill, mirroring the gh_pr_review_parse contract by printing resolved key=value args or usage errors with no runtime side effects.

nodes.name: devx_pr_review_all_parse
nodes.summary: Parses and validates the devx:pr-review-all skill's CLI arguments (PR#, remote, --defer-reply/--no-reply), emitting one key=value line per resolved arg on success or an error to stderr on invalid input.

nodes.name: dot_help.sh
nodes.summary: Implements the `dot-help` command, rendering the dotfiles project's overview, setup, features, commands, and docs sections through ux_lib.

nodes.name: _dot_help_section_rows
nodes.summary: Maps a dot-help section name (overview/setup/mounts/features/commands/docs, with aliases) to its row-rendering function, erroring on an unrecognized section.

nodes.name: dot_help
nodes.summary: Public dot-help entry point; routes to the usage summary, section list, full documentation dump, or a single section's rows based on the first argument.

nodes.name: dotfiles_backup.sh
nodes.summary: SSOT for the dotfiles fixed-suffix backup policy (issue #806): defines DOTFILES_BACKUP_SUFFIX/DOTFILES_ORIGINAL_SUFFIX and copy/move primitives that overwrite the previous backup instead of accumulating timestamped files.

nodes.name: dotfiles_backup_copy
nodes.summary: Copies a target file or directory to its fixed backup path, overwriting any prior backup and handling files versus directories distinctly to avoid nested-copy corruption.

nodes.name: dotfiles_backup_move
nodes.summary: Moves a target file or directory to its fixed backup path, overwriting any prior backup; no-op when the target does not exist.

nodes.name: dotfiles_root.sh
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes $DOTFILES_ROOT to the main git worktree instead of a linked worktree, preventing dangling Claude Code config symlinks after a linked worktree is removed (issue #589).

nodes.name: _resolve_dotfiles_root_canonical
nodes.summary: Given a candidate directory, prints the main-worktree path when the candidate is a linked git worktree, falling back safely to the candidate itself in every edge case (no git, non-worktree, opt-out env var, resolution failure).

nodes.name: _dotfiles_root_canonicalize
nodes.summary: In-place re-exports DOTFILES_ROOT and SHELL_COMMON to their canonical main-worktree path when they differ; a safe no-op otherwise.

nodes.name: ensure_ollama_deps_wrapper.sh
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper function that locates and delegates to the external ensure-ollama-deps.sh dependency-check script.

nodes.name: ensure_ollama_deps
nodes.summary: Resolves the ensure-ollama-deps.sh script path via SHELL_COMMON (with a $HOME/dotfiles fallback) and executes it with bash, erroring out with a diagnostic hint if the script is missing.

nodes.name: fasd.sh
nodes.summary: fasd (frecency-based directory/file jumper) help documentation library, rendering usage sections (core, ranking, patterns, advanced, use-cases, tips, comparisons, integration, troubleshooting) via ux_lib.

nodes.name: _fasd_help_section_rows
nodes.summary: Maps a fasd-help section name to its corresponding row-rendering function among the documented sections.

nodes.name: _fasd_help_full
nodes.summary: Renders every fasd-help section in sequence to print the complete fasd documentation.

nodes.name: fasd_help
nodes.summary: Public fasd-help entry point; shows the usage summary, section list, full doc, or a single section based on the argument.

nodes.name: fd.sh
nodes.summary: fd (fast file-finder) help documentation library, rendering usage sections (concept, basic patterns, type/case/extension/depth/scope filters, exec, examples, comparisons) via ux_lib.

nodes.name: _fd_help_section_rows
nodes.summary: Maps an fd-help section name to its corresponding row-rendering function among the documented sections.

nodes.name: _fd_help_full
nodes.summary: Renders every fd-help section in sequence to print the complete fd documentation.

nodes.name: fd_help
nodes.summary: Public fd-help entry point; shows the usage summary, section list, full doc, or a single section based on the argument.

nodes.name: file_cleanup.sh
nodes.summary: Interactive cleanup command (`del-file` / `clean-home`) for removing backup/original/cache files matching configurable patterns, with preview, batch-delete, and one-by-one confirmation modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Sourced by bash/zsh only (never dash) despite the POSIX shebang, so it can safely use bash/zsh array syntax; an `emulate -L sh` hook keeps it legal when sourced from zsh.

nodes.name: _cleanup_collect_matches
nodes.summary: Collects filesystem entries matching the active cleanup patterns into a result array for preview or deletion.

nodes.name: _cleanup_preview
nodes.summary: Renders a preview table of matched backup/original/cache files with sizes before any destructive action is taken.

nodes.name: _cleanup_delete_all
nodes.summary: Deletes every matched file/directory in one batch after confirmation.

nodes.name: _cleanup_delete_individually
nodes.summary: Walks matched files one at a time, prompting the user to confirm or skip each deletion individually.

nodes.name: del_file_help
nodes.summary: Prints usage/help text for the del-file / clean-home command.

nodes.name: del_file
nodes.summary: Main entry point for del-file/clean-home; selects the target pattern set, collects matches, and dispatches to preview/select/delete-all/delete-individually based on flags.

nodes.name: fzf.sh
nodes.summary: fzf (fuzzy finder) help documentation library, rendering usage sections (keybindings, navigation, editing, selection, actions, preview, examples, config) via ux_lib.

nodes.name: _fzf_help_section_rows

nodes.name: gcp_scan.sh

nodes.languageNotes: Uses `git cherry-pick -n` as a preflight probe rather than file-diff heuristics, so conflict/no-op prediction is immune to context-drift from comment rewrites or refactors that broke earlier approaches (issue #913).

nodes.name: _gcp_scan_conflict_adds_new_content

nodes.name: _gcp_scan_predict_content_conflict
nodes.summary: Predicts whether a candidate commit will produce a real content conflict against the base branch ahead of actually executing the cherry-pick.

nodes.name: _gcp_scan
nodes.summary: Main `gcp scan` implementation: walks commits missing from the base branch, runs preflight/skip-list/conflict-prediction checks on each, displays an interactive candidate list excluding no-ops, and drives the cherry-pick execution loop.

nodes.summary: Quick tmux attach/switch helper that jumps to the first available tmux session, attaching when outside tmux or switching the client when already inside one.

nodes.name: tmux_help

nodes.name: tmux_teardown
nodes.summary: Kills all tmux sessions or a specific named session, listing running sessions on error.

nodes.name: ux_help.sh

nodes.name: _ux_help_full

nodes.name: window_help.sh

nodes.name: _work_help_full

nodes.name: _work_log_help_full

nodes.name: wsl_check.sh
nodes.summary: WSL and Docker environment health check that reports the Windows host C: drive, WSL root disk, memory, CPU, and Docker reclaimable space, with a one-line summary, a detailed --all report, and an opt-in interactive prune.
nodes.languageNotes: Surfaces the Windows host C: drive metric first because a prior incident (issue #897) showed WSL can crash from host-drive exhaustion even while the WSL disk itself has headroom; also guards every destructive prune behind an interactive-shell check.

nodes.name: wsl_check
nodes.summary: Dispatcher routing to the one-line summary (default), the full report (--all), or the full report followed by an interactive prune (--prune / --prune-all).

nodes.name: _wsl_check_oneline
nodes.summary: Builds the compact single-line status string (c-drive, disk, cpu, mem, docker) with per-segment color severity, used as the default wsl-check output.

nodes.name: _wsl_check_full
nodes.summary: Renders the detailed multi-section health report (C: drive, WSL disk, vhdx compaction, memory, Docker reclaimable space) and triggers auto-prune when enabled and over threshold.

nodes.name: _wsl_check_prune
nodes.summary: Single chokepoint for destructive Docker pruning (builder-cache/dangling-image safe set, or full system prune), refusing to run in non-interactive shells.

nodes.name: wsl_check_help.sh
nodes.summary: SSOT help dispatcher for wsl-check, describing its one-line summary mode, --all detailed report, and --prune/--prune-all destructive-cleanup flags.

nodes.name: wsl_check_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for wsl-check-help, currently always rendering the single summary view.

nodes.name: _wsl_check_help_summary
nodes.summary: Prints wsl-check usage, modes, thresholds, and destructive-flag warnings.

nodes.name: zsh.sh
nodes.summary: Zsh shell management functions covering theme/plugin listing and switching, config reload, snippet editing, p10k cache clearing, VS Code prompt fixes, and git repositoryformatversion repair after worktree teardown.

nodes.name: zsh_theme
nodes.summary: Changes the active oh-my-zsh theme by rewriting the ZSH_THEME line in ~/.zshrc.

nodes.name: zsh_plugins
nodes.summary: Lists installed custom and built-in oh-my-zsh plugins alongside a curated list of popular plugin suggestions.

nodes.name: zsh_snippet
nodes.summary: Creates or opens a named config snippet under ~/.zshrc.d/ in the user's editor.

nodes.name: _zsh_clear_p10k_caches
nodes.summary: SSOT cleaner that removes every powerlevel10k cache variant (instant-prompt, compiled dump, and per-user scratch dir) and reports how many artifacts were removed, fixing a stale-prompt bug from worktree-spawn races (issue #705).

nodes.name: zsh_fix_vscode
nodes.summary: Clears the stale VS Code integrated-terminal zcompdump plus p10k caches to restore the p10k prompt after a VS Code update reverts it to the default HOSTNAME% prompt.

nodes.name: zsh_git_fix
nodes.summary: Restores .git/config after all worktrees are removed by clearing extensions.worktreeConfig and resetting repositoryformatversion, working around a gitstatusd bug that breaks p10k's branch display (issue #968).

nodes.name: zsh_autosuggestions.sh
nodes.summary: Help and installation-status reporting for the zsh-autosuggestions plugin, covering key bindings, matching strategies, environment variables, and customization snippets.

nodes.name: zsh_autosuggestions_help
nodes.summary: Dispatcher for zsh-autosuggestions-help, routing to summary, section-list, full, or a specific section (keys, strategies, status, etc.).

nodes.name: zsh_autosuggestions_install_help
nodes.summary: Prints the installation-flow description (clone, register in plugins array, configure keybindings) for the zsh-autosuggestions installer wrapper.

nodes.name: zsh_help.sh
nodes.summary: SSOT help dispatcher aggregating zsh shell management commands (switching, config, theme, plugins, packages, troubleshooting) into a single sectioned reference.

nodes.name: zsh_help
nodes.summary: Dispatcher for zsh-help, routing to summary, section-list, full, or a specific section.

nodes.name: _zsh_help_full
nodes.summary: Renders every zsh-help section in sequence for the --all view.

nodes.name: zz_help_standard_adapter.sh
nodes.summary: Adapter that retrofits dozens of legacy topic `*_help` functions (git, tmux, zsh, work, ux, etc.) to the shared section/--list/--all interface without editing each original: it clones the original function under `_help_std_orig_*` via eval and wraps the public name with a standard dispatcher, skipping functions already guideline-compliant. The `zz_` filename prefix is this project's load-last convention, so it runs after every topic help function in shell-common/functions/ has been defined.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses eval-based dynamic function definition plus typeset -f/declare -f introspection to clone and wrap existing shell functions at load time — a metaprogramming pattern uncommon in POSIX shell, kept portable between bash and zsh via ZSH_VERSION branching.

nodes.name: apply_help_standard_adapter
nodes.summary: Module entry point invoked at load time: registers default help descriptions if available, then wraps every topic in the hardcoded topic list.

nodes.name: _help_std_wrap_topics
nodes.summary: Iterates a hardcoded ~50-topic list (git, tmux, zsh, work, work_log, ux, sys, gpu, network, dir, and more) and calls _help_std_wrap_one on each `<topic>_help` function.

nodes.name: _help_std_wrap_one
nodes.summary: Wraps a single `<topic>_help` function with the standard interface unless it is already wrapped or guideline-compliant, also defining the zsh dash-alias function and a plain alias.

nodes.name: _help_std_define_wrapper
nodes.summary: Generates and eval's the summary/list/full/dispatcher function set that replaces a legacy help function's name, delegating unmatched cases to the cloned original.

nodes.name: dmc.sh
nodes.summary: Defines dmc-playground project shell functions for running the FastAPI/Uvicorn backend across dev, test, and prod environments and for invoking the project's API and database CLI tools against environment-specific Postgres connection URLs.
nodes.languageNotes: POSIX sh with the repo's standard interactive guard and _have/_need command-existence helpers duplicated per project file.

nodes.name: run_bes
nodes.summary: Starts the dmc-playground FastAPI backend in dev mode via `uv run uvicorn` with auto-reload on the dev host/port.

nodes.name: run_tbes
nodes.summary: Starts the dmc-playground backend against the test host/port with PYTEST_CURRENT_TEST set and auto-reload enabled.

nodes.name: run_pbes
nodes.summary: Starts the dmc-playground backend in production mode (no reload) via plain `uvicorn` bound to the prod host/port.

nodes.name: run_api_cli
nodes.summary: Invokes the project's `api_cli.py` against the dev API URL, or an override passed as the first argument.

nodes.name: run_tapi_cli
nodes.summary: Invokes `api_cli.py` against the test API URL, or an override argument.

nodes.name: run_papi_cli
nodes.summary: Invokes `api_cli.py` against the production API URL, or an override argument.

nodes.name: run_db_cli
nodes.summary: Invokes the project's `db_cli.py` against the dev Postgres connection URL, or an override argument.

nodes.name: run_tdb_cli
nodes.summary: Invokes `db_cli.py` against the test Postgres connection URL, or an override argument.

nodes.name: run_pdb_cli
nodes.summary: Invokes `db_cli.py` against the production Postgres connection URL, or an override argument.

nodes.name: finrx.sh
nodes.summary: Defines a single shell shortcut for the FinRx project that forwards all arguments to the ticker-library CLI script.
nodes.languageNotes: Minimal POSIX sh wrapper following the same interactive-guard and _have/_need helper pattern used across shell-common/projects/*.sh.

nodes.name: run_fr_cli
nodes.summary: Requires `python` and forwards all passed arguments to the FinRx `cli.py` entry script under `src/ticker_library/cli/`.

nodes.name: smithery.sh
nodes.summary: Defines smithery-playground project shell functions for running its FastAPI/Uvicorn service across dev, test, and prod environments on dedicated ports.
nodes.languageNotes: Same dev/test/prod Uvicorn launch pattern as dmc.sh but without a database CLI layer.

nodes.name: run_smt
nodes.summary: Starts the smithery-playground service in dev mode via `uv run uvicorn` with auto-reload on the dev host/port.

nodes.name: run_tsmt
nodes.summary: Starts the smithery-playground service against the test host/port with PYTEST_CURRENT_TEST set and auto-reload enabled.

nodes.name: run_psmt
nodes.summary: Starts the smithery-playground service in production mode (no reload) via plain `uvicorn` bound to the prod host/port.

nodes.name: check_uv.sh
nodes.summary: Explicitly-invoked diagnostic tool that inspects uv (Python package manager) configuration, config files, environment variables, and index connectivity to troubleshoot corporate-proxy/Nexus setups.
nodes.languageNotes: Parses uv.toml with sed since uv has no CLI config query command; sources a shared init.sh to bootstrap SHELL_COMMON/ux_lib before running.

nodes.name: check_uv_config
nodes.summary: Prints the installed uv version, location, and active native-tls/extra-index-url settings read from uv.toml.

nodes.name: check_uv_config_files
nodes.summary: Checks whether ~/.config/uv/uv.toml is a symlink to the dotfiles-managed environment variant and reports its content or suggests re-running setup.sh.

nodes.name: check_uv_environment
nodes.summary: Reports proxy, CA/SSL, and UV_* environment variables relevant to uv's network behavior.

nodes.name: check_uv_connectivity
nodes.summary: Tests HTTP reachability of every configured extra-index-url (from uv.toml and UV_EXTRA_INDEX_URL) using curl and reports success/failure with likely causes.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the config/files/env/connectivity sub-checks based on the first CLI argument, defaulting to running all of them.

nodes.name: check_ux_consistency.sh
nodes.summary: Explicitly-invoked linter that scans bash/app, bash/alias, and bash/coreutils for deprecated raw tput color codes and verifies ux_lib's Python helper scripts remain executable.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Greps for deprecated tput color definitions across bash script directories and checks that ux_lib's Python scripts are executable, returning a nonzero exit if issues are found.

nodes.name: cp_wdown.sh
nodes.summary: Bash-only implementation (relies on mapfile, compgen, (( ))) that copies files from the Windows Downloads folder into WSL, resolving the Windows path via pwsh.exe/powershell.exe/cmd.exe fallbacks; invoked through the shell-common/functions/cp_wdown.sh wrapper rather than sourced directly.

nodes.name: cp_wdown
nodes.summary: Parses getopts flags for destination/source/recursive/force/verbose/dry-run, locates the Windows Downloads directory across three PowerShell/cmd fallbacks, converts it via wslpath, then globs and copies matching files into the WSL destination.

nodes.name: delete_claude.sh
nodes.summary: Explicitly-invoked interactive uninstaller that removes the Claude Code binary, runtime cache, and session data while preserving projects/downloads/settings, with fallback removal for Homebrew/WinGet/NPM installs.

nodes.name: delete_claude
nodes.summary: Confirms with the user, then removes the Claude Code binary, runtime data, and session cache directories, detects and optionally removes Homebrew/WinGet/NPM installations, and prints a removal summary with reinstall instructions.

nodes.name: demo_ux.sh

nodes.name: demo_colors
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's semantic color functions and text style variables (bold, dim, muted).

nodes.name: demo_headers
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's header and section formatting helpers.

nodes.name: demo_lists
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's bulleted and numbered list rendering helpers.

nodes.name: demo_progress
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's progress bar and step indicator rendering.

nodes.name: demo_interactive
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's interactive input prompts (confirm, text input) with sample flows.

nodes.name: demo_menu
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's menu-rendering helper with sample options.

nodes.name: demo_rich_progress
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's richer progress-tracking display variant.

nodes.name: demo_log_filtering
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's log-level filtering and formatted log output helpers.

nodes.name: demo_usage
nodes.summary: Demonstrates ux_lib's usage/help-text formatting helper.

nodes.name: demo_real_world
nodes.summary: Demonstrates a realistic combined ux_lib usage scenario mixing headers, progress, and status output.

nodes.name: show_menu
nodes.summary: Renders the top-level interactive demo menu listing each available demo section.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Drives the interactive demo loop, dispatching user menu selections to the individual demo_* functions until the user exits.

nodes.name: devx.sh

nodes.name: _devx_load_ux_lib
nodes.summary: Loads ux_lib.sh from SHELL_COMMON if the ux_info function is not already defined, tolerating its absence.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Resolves DOTFILES_ROOT/SHELL_COMMON from the script's own location, loads ux_lib, then sources and calls the real devx function from shell-common/functions/devx.sh.

nodes.name: docker_configure_proxy.sh

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Walks the user through confirming setup, entering proxy details, writing a systemd drop-in config for Docker, restarting the service, and verifying/testing the proxy with a docker pull.

nodes.name: enable_docker.sh

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: ensure-ollama-deps.sh

nodes.languageNotes: Defines minimal inline fallback ux_* functions if ux_lib.sh cannot be located, so the script degrades gracefully instead of failing.

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: ensure_jq.sh

nodes.name: ensure_jq
nodes.summary: Checks whether jq is on PATH and, if not, installs it via the detected package manager, reporting success/failure.

nodes.name: analyze_bash_scripts.sh
nodes.summary: Scans a directory of bash files and prints a Markdown-style inventory of every alias and function defined within, skipping lib/ paths; a tools/custom/ script invoked explicitly rather than auto-sourced.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints usage instructions and example invocations for analyze_bash_scripts.sh.

nodes.name: parse_aliases
nodes.summary: Extracts unique alias definitions from a given file into the global ALL_ALIASES map, skipping comments and duplicates.

nodes.name: parse_functions
nodes.summary: Extracts unique bash function names (both `name()` and `function name()` forms) from a given file into ALL_FUNCTIONS.

nodes.name: print_results
nodes.summary: Renders the collected alias and function inventory as a UX-formatted report via ux_lib.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Validates the target directory argument, recursively finds all .sh files excluding lib/, then parses and reports their aliases and functions.

nodes.name: check_apt.sh
nodes.summary: Diagnoses apt/APT package manager configuration, config files, environment variables, and network connectivity to catch proxy or repository misconfigurations before an install fails; invoked explicitly, not auto-sourced.

nodes.name: check_apt_config
nodes.summary: Checks active apt configuration for proxy, retry, and timeout settings via `apt-config dump`.

nodes.name: check_apt_config_files
nodes.summary: Scans known apt config file locations (/etc/apt/apt.conf.d/, apt.conf) for proxy and auth directives and reports which files define them.

nodes.name: check_apt_environment
nodes.summary: Reports http_proxy/https_proxy/no_proxy environment variables relevant to apt operations.

nodes.name: check_apt_connectivity
nodes.summary: Tests reachability of apt package repositories (e.g. archive.ubuntu.com) to confirm network/proxy access works end-to-end.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Runs the full apt diagnostic suite in sequence and prints a pass/fail summary.

nodes.name: check_cargo.sh
nodes.summary: Diagnoses Rust/Cargo package manager configuration, config files, environment variables, and registry connectivity to help debug cargo install/build failures in restricted networks; invoked explicitly, not auto-sourced.

nodes.name: check_cargo_config
nodes.summary: Checks active cargo configuration (registries, proxy settings) via `cargo config get` or config.toml.

nodes.name: check_cargo_config_files
nodes.summary: Scans known cargo config file locations (~/.cargo/config.toml, project-level config) for registry and proxy directives.

nodes.name: check_cargo_environment
nodes.summary: Reports CARGO_HOME and proxy-related environment variables affecting cargo.

nodes.name: check_cargo_connectivity
nodes.summary: Tests reachability of crates.io and the cargo registry index to confirm cargo can fetch dependencies.

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: claude_init
nodes.summary: Bootstraps a fresh Claude Code environment: installs the CLI, links config, and runs first-time setup steps.

nodes.name: claude_edit_settings
nodes.summary: Opens the active account's Claude settings.json in the configured editor.

nodes.name: open_claude_plugins
nodes.summary: Opens the Claude Code plugins directory in the configured editor or file manager.

nodes.name: cltest
nodes.summary: Runs a quick smoke test invocation of the claude CLI to confirm it is functional.

nodes.name: clskip
nodes.summary: Launches Claude Code in skip-permissions mode for a quick, unattended session.

nodes.name: claude_yolo
nodes.summary: Launches Claude Code with all permission prompts bypassed (YOLO mode), exporting the settings needed to enable it and restoring prior settings afterward.

nodes.name: _claude_resolve_account
nodes.summary: Determines which configured Claude account (email/config dir) should be active based on the current PC setup mode and environment.

nodes.name: _claude_ensure_symlink
nodes.summary: Ensures the account-specific Claude config directory is correctly symlinked into the active Claude config location.

nodes.name: _claude_ensure_settings_copy
nodes.summary: Reconciles the per-account settings.json real-file copy with the tracked SSOT, migrating any user-written /model overrides into settings.local.json.

nodes.name: _claude_compose_skills_dir
nodes.summary: Builds the composed skills directory by symlinking skills from the dotfiles repo and any configured plugin sources for the active account.

nodes.name: _claude_account_setup_one
nodes.summary: Runs the one-time setup steps (symlinks, settings copy) for a single Claude account.

nodes.name: claude_accounts_status
nodes.summary: Prints the current status of all configured Claude accounts, including OAuth state and active account.

nodes.name: claude_accounts_init
nodes.summary: Initializes multi-account configuration directories for Claude Code.

nodes.name: claude_accounts_migrate
nodes.summary: Migrates an existing single-account Claude Code setup into the multi-account directory layout.

nodes.name: claude_accounts_rollback

nodes.name: claude_accounts_repair

nodes.name: claude_accounts
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the claude_accounts_* subcommands (status/init/migrate/rollback/repair) based on the first argument.

nodes.name: codex.sh

nodes.name: _codex_home_dir
nodes.summary: Resolves the active Codex home directory, accounting for multi-account setup mode.

nodes.name: _codex_skills_fingerprint
nodes.summary: Computes a fingerprint of the current skills source tree used to detect whether a re-sync is needed.

nodes.name: _codex_skills_has_legacy_layout
nodes.summary: Detects whether the Codex skills directory still uses the pre-migration legacy symlink layout.

nodes.name: codex_skills_sync_if_needed
nodes.summary: Runs the Codex skills sync script only when the fingerprint indicates the skills tree has changed.

nodes.name: _codex_register_auto_sync_hooks
nodes.summary: Registers shell hooks that trigger periodic background skills auto-sync on new interactive shells.

nodes.name: _codex_maybe_auto_sync
nodes.summary: Runs the periodic auto-sync check when the configured interval has elapsed since the last sync.

nodes.name: codex_status
nodes.summary: Prints the current Codex CLI installation and skills-sync status.

nodes.name: codex
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around the codex CLI binary, ensuring auto-sync runs before invocation.

nodes.name: docker.sh
nodes.summary: Docker and Docker Compose helper functions and aliases: log tailing with compose/container fallback, restart-with-auto-discovery, cleanup (stop/remove/prune all), env inspection, and image export.

nodes.name: dcl
nodes.summary: Tails logs for a compose service or bare container name, trying docker compose logs first and falling back to docker logs.

nodes.name: dcr
nodes.summary: Restarts a compose service by auto-discovering its compose file (current dir, then known project paths), falling back to a plain docker restart.

nodes.name: dbash
nodes.summary: Opens an interactive shell into a running container, preferring bash and falling back to sh.

nodes.name: dstopall
nodes.summary: Stops all currently running containers.

nodes.name: drmall
nodes.summary: Removes all stopped containers.

nodes.name: drm_dangling
nodes.summary: Removes dangling (untagged) Docker images.

nodes.name: dprune_full
nodes.summary: Runs a full Docker system prune covering containers, images, networks, and volumes.

nodes.name: dvol_rm_dangling
nodes.summary: Removes dangling (unattached) Docker volumes.

nodes.name: denv
nodes.summary: Inspects and prints the environment variables configured for a running container.

nodes.name: dlog_last
nodes.summary: Shows the last N lines of logs for a given container.

nodes.name: dexport
nodes.summary: Exports a running container's filesystem as a tarball for backup or inspection.

nodes.name: dproxy_show
nodes.summary: Displays the proxy environment variables currently configured for the Docker daemon.

nodes.name: nvm.sh
nodes.summary: Auto-sources NVM (Node Version Manager) in interactive shells only and exposes an nvm-install wrapper, guarded to keep non-interactive shell startup cost at zero.

nodes.name: nvm_install
