nodes.name: ollama.sh
nodes.summary: Hybrid Ollama integration unifying WSL-native and Docker-based Ollama backends behind one command interface, with backend auto-detection, status reporting, and systemd restart handling.

nodes.name: ollama_backend_detect
nodes.summary: Auto-detects whether Ollama is running natively on WSL or inside a Docker container.

nodes.name: ollama_backend_status
nodes.summary: Reports the running/stopped status of the detected Ollama backend.

nodes.name: ollama_cmd
nodes.summary: Dispatches an ollama subcommand to the correct backend (native WSL or Docker exec).

nodes.name: ollama_api_base_url
nodes.summary: Resolves the correct Ollama API base URL depending on the active backend.

nodes.name: ollama_status_env
nodes.summary: Prints the resolved Ollama environment configuration (backend, base URL, ports).

nodes.name: ollama_status
nodes.summary: Prints a combined status report covering backend detection, service state, and API reachability.

nodes.name: ollama_models
nodes.summary: Lists models currently pulled/available in the active Ollama backend.

nodes.name: ollama_systemd_restart
nodes.summary: Restarts the native Ollama systemd service, with WSL-specific handling.

nodes.name: opencode.sh
nodes.summary: OpenCode CLI setup and workflow helpers: install verification, config editing (symlinked per environment), a smoke test command, uninstall, and a structured help system.

nodes.name: opencode_verify
nodes.summary: Runs a full verification pass over the OpenCode installation, checking binary presence, config symlinks, and environment setup.

nodes.name: opencode_edit
nodes.summary: Opens the active OpenCode configuration file in the configured editor.

nodes.name: uninstall_opencode
nodes.summary: Uninstalls the OpenCode CLI and removes its associated configuration symlinks.

nodes.name: opencode_help
nodes.summary: Entry point for the OpenCode integration help system.

nodes.name: opentest
nodes.summary: Runs a quick smoke test invocation of the opencode CLI to confirm it is functional.

nodes.name: pip.sh
nodes.summary: POSIX-compatible alias module for Python package management (pip install/freeze/uninstall/check) plus ruff/mypy code-quality shortcuts; no functions, aliases only.

nodes.name: postgresql.sh
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL service bootstrap and helper subsystem: registered-service persistence, user/database provisioning with identifier validation and password-strength checks, pg_service.conf generation, and a bootstrap/sync workflow for reproducible local Postgres setups.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses bash strict mode (set -euo pipefail) and dedicated identifier/password validation helpers to guard against SQL injection and weak credentials in generated service configs.

nodes.name: _admin_sql
nodes.summary: Runs a SQL statement against Postgres using admin/superuser credentials, shared by the higher-level psql_* provisioning functions.

nodes.name: _validate_identifier
nodes.summary: Validates that a proposed database/user identifier is safe to interpolate into SQL (alphanumeric/underscore only).

nodes.name: _register_aliases
nodes.summary: Registers a new service's name and connection URI into the persisted services list and pg_service.conf.

nodes.name: psql_add
nodes.summary: Interactively provisions a new PostgreSQL user and database and registers it as a named service.

nodes.name: psql_list
nodes.summary: Lists all registered PostgreSQL services and their connection details.

nodes.name: psql_del
nodes.summary: Removes a registered PostgreSQL service, optionally dropping its underlying user and database.

nodes.name: psql_user
nodes.summary: Creates, alters, or drops a PostgreSQL user role with validated identifiers and password strength checks.

nodes.name: psql_db
nodes.summary: Creates, alters, or drops a PostgreSQL database with an optional owner assignment.

nodes.name: psql_bootstrap
nodes.summary: Bootstraps a full local PostgreSQL setup: user, database, and service registration in one guided flow.

nodes.name: psql_sync
nodes.summary: Synchronizes the persisted services list and pg_service.conf so both stay consistent with actual server state.

nodes.name: pyenv.sh
nodes.summary: Initializes pyenv (root path, PATH, shell hook with --no-rehash to avoid VSCode multi-terminal lock races) and exposes install/uninstall wrappers.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately skips automatic pyenv-rehash on every shell start to avoid lock contention when many terminals launch concurrently (e.g. VS Code), rehashing only after explicit installs.

nodes.name: py_uninstall
nodes.summary: Uninstalls a specified Python version via pyenv and cleans up related shims.

nodes.name: python.sh
nodes.summary: Defines POSIX-compatible aliases for Python venv workflows (create, activate, deactivate, remove) in both long and short forms.

nodes.name: redis.sh
nodes.summary: Redis service bootstrap and CLI wrapper functions for WSL, providing server control, ping/info/monitor commands, key scanning, and safe flush confirmations via ux_lib.

nodes.name: redis_server
nodes.summary: Starts, stops, restarts, or reports status of the Redis service, dispatching to systemctl or the legacy service command depending on init system.

nodes.name: redis_ping
nodes.summary: Pings a Redis host/port and reports success or failure, hinting at REDISCLI_AUTH when no password is set.

nodes.name: redis_flush
nodes.summary: Flushes the current database or all Redis databases after an explicit confirmation prompt to avoid accidental data loss.

nodes.name: sync_to_deploy.sh
nodes.summary: Merges an internal and external main branch on a temporary branch and force-pushes the result to a named deploy branch, with whitelist validation, conflict recovery instructions, and automatic cleanup via trap.

nodes.name: sync_to_deploy
nodes.summary: Main entry point that validates the deploy branch against a whitelist, fetches internal/external main branches, merges them on a temp branch, and force-with-lease pushes to the deploy branch with trap-based cleanup on any exit path.
nodes.languageNotes: Defined as a subshell function `name() ( ... )` rather than `name() { ... }`, isolating its cwd/trap changes from the caller's shell.

nodes.name: _sync_to_deploy_usage
nodes.summary: Prints usage help and the environment variable overrides table for sync-to-deploy.

nodes.name: _sync_to_deploy_in_whitelist
nodes.summary: Checks whether a target deploy branch name is present in the space-separated whitelist, treating an empty whitelist as allow-all.

nodes.name: uv.sh
nodes.summary: Defines short-form aliases for common uv (Python package manager) operations — sync, upgrade, dev install, lock, requirements export/sync — plus an install wrapper function.

nodes.name: zsh.sh
nodes.summary: Bash-specific zsh integration: installs zsh/oh-my-zsh, and defines quick aliases for shell switching and zsh config inspection, guarded to only load under bash.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `export -f` (bash-only) so it early-returns when not running under bash, mirroring the POSIX-safe counterpart in shell-common/functions/zsh.sh.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Module-context guide for the ux_lib UX system: purpose, operational commands (demo, consistency check, lint), standard usage patterns, golden rules (no raw echo/printf/tput), and dependency notes.

nodes.name: UX_GUIDELINES.md
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for dotfiles terminal UX: design principles, color semantics, a function-structure template, file locations, portability instructions for reuse in other projects, and testing/best-practice guidance.

nodes.name: ux_lib.sh
nodes.summary: Central POSIX-compliant UX library providing semantic colors, headers/success/error/warning output, spinners and progress bars, confirmation/input/menu prompts, table formatting, error trapping, and log filtering — the single styling dependency for all dotfiles scripts.
nodes.languageNotes: Detects bash vs zsh at load time to resolve its own directory (BASH_SOURCE vs ${0:h}) so the library remains portable and self-contained across shells.

nodes.name: ux_get_safe_script_name
nodes.summary: Safely extracts the script's basename from $0 using parameter expansion only, preventing flag-injection errors when sourced under flags like `bash -i -l`.

nodes.name: ux_header
nodes.summary: Prints a bold, colored section header used to open the output of a command or operation.

nodes.name: ux_section
nodes.summary: Prints a subsection title beneath a header, used to group related output lines.

nodes.name: ux_info
nodes.summary: Prints an informational message in the semantic info color, the standard way to relay status/tips to the user.

nodes.name: ux_spinner
nodes.summary: Renders an animated terminal spinner tied to a background process PID, providing visual feedback during long-running operations.

nodes.name: ux_with_spinner
nodes.summary: Runs an arbitrary command in the background while displaying a spinner, then reports success or failure based on its exit code.

nodes.name: ux_with_progress
nodes.summary: Runs a command while showing a step-based progress indicator instead of a spinner.

nodes.name: ux_confirm
nodes.summary: Prompts the user for a yes/no confirmation with a configurable default, used to gate destructive operations.

nodes.name: ux_input
nodes.summary: Prompts the user for free-text input with an optional default value.

nodes.name: ux_menu
nodes.summary: Renders a numbered selection menu from a list of options and returns the user's chosen index/value.

nodes.name: ux_table_row
nodes.summary: Prints one aligned row of a two/three-column terminal table.

nodes.name: ux_table_header
nodes.summary: Prints the header row and separator line for a terminal table, pairing with ux_table_row for the body.

nodes.name: ux_divider
nodes.summary: Prints a thin horizontal divider line sized to the terminal width.

nodes.name: ux_divider_thick
nodes.summary: Prints a bold/thick horizontal divider line, used to separate major output sections.

nodes.name: ux_require
nodes.summary: Asserts that a required command or condition is present, printing a formatted error and returning non-zero when it is missing.

nodes.name: ux_usage
nodes.summary: Prints a standardized usage line (command name, arguments, description) used by nearly every dotfiles CLI helper for its no-args help output.

nodes.name: ux_filter_logs
nodes.summary: Filters raw log/command output down to lines relevant to error diagnosis, reducing noise before display.

nodes.name: loader.sh
nodes.summary: Module loader shared by bash and zsh entry points: loads all `.sh` files in a category directory (skipping `.local.sh` overrides) and auto-loads shell-specific directories while honoring a configurable skip list.

nodes.name: load_category
nodes.summary: Sources every `.sh` file in a given shell-common category directory (e.g. env, aliases), skipping `.local.sh` user overrides and emitting debug logs when DEBUG_DOTFILES is set.

nodes.name: load_auto_directories
nodes.summary: Iterates subdirectories of a base directory and sources all files matching a given extension, skipping directories listed in the loader skip-list config.

nodes.name: path_resolver.sh
nodes.summary: Centralizes DOTFILES_ROOT detection so both bash and zsh entry points resolve the dotfiles installation directory the same way, avoiding duplicated path logic.

nodes.name: resolve_dotfiles_root
nodes.summary: Detects the calling script's location (via BASH_SOURCE or zsh's %N) and walks up to find the dotfiles root, validating candidates by checking for a shell-common subdirectory, with a final HOME/dotfiles fallback.

nodes.name: safe_source.sh
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for safely sourcing shell files with a success counter, distinguishing critical paths (functions/env/integrations) that must report errors from optional ones that fail silently.

nodes.name: safe_source
nodes.summary: Sources a file directly into the caller's shell (never via a subshell, to preserve function/alias definitions), increments a global counter on success, and reports errors selectively based on the file's path and DEBUG_DOTFILES.

nodes.name: setup_mode.sh
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for reading the ~/.dotfiles-setup-mode file and auto-clearing inherited corporate proxy environment variables on public/external PC modes.

nodes.name: _apply_setup_mode_config
nodes.summary: Reads the setup-mode marker file and unsets proxy environment variables when the mode indicates a public/external machine, leaving internal-mode proxy configuration to proxy.local.sh.

nodes.name: gh_pr_merge_board_gate.sh
nodes.summary: Bats test fixture mirroring the Step 4-B project-board approval gate snippet in the gh-pr-merge skill, verifying merges are refused unless the board Status is 'Approved' (or the check is skipped).

nodes.name: gh_pr_merge_board_gate
nodes.summary: Gates a PR merge on the project board Status field, refusing (rc=2) unless the card is 'Approved', empty (no board), or the GH_PR_MERGE_SKIP_BOARD_CHECK escape hatch is set.

nodes.name: gh_pr_reply_auto_approve.sh
nodes.summary: Bats test fixture mirroring the gh-pr-reply skill's Step 8 solo-repo auto-approve gating logic — four guard checks (allowlist, PR state/draft, reviewDecision) that decide whether to bypass the fail-closed approval guard.

nodes.name: gh_pr_reply_auto_approve_step8
nodes.summary: Evaluates four guards (comment count, repo allowlist membership, PR state/draft, reviewDecision) and, if all pass, calls the board-sync helper to auto-approve, recording the outcome in STEP8_OUTCOME.

nodes.name: gh_pr_reply_render_step8_row
nodes.summary: Maps a STEP8_OUTCOME value to its user-visible report row (OK/SKIP/WARN/FAIL) for the gh-pr-reply final summary.

nodes.name: gh_pr_reply_board_sync.sh
nodes.summary: Bats test fixture mirroring the gh-pr-reply skill's Step 6.5 board-sync snippet, which returns a PR's board card to 'In review' after fix commits are pushed.

nodes.name: gh_pr_reply_board_sync_step65
nodes.summary: Moves the PR's board card back to 'In review' when at least one fix commit was pushed, no-op otherwise, always soft-failing (returns 0).

nodes.name: gh_pr_review_arg_parse.sh
nodes.summary: Thin bats fixture wrapper that force-loads the production gh_pr_review_parse SSOT (shell-common/functions/gh_pr_review.sh) under its historical fixture load path so existing tests keep working after issue #664 moved the logic to production code.

nodes.name: gh_pr_stacked_detect.sh
nodes.summary: Bats test fixture mirroring the gh-pr skill's Stage 1/2 stacked-PR detection logic: repo-signal detection, CLI arg parsing (--no-stack/--base), and parent-PR candidate discovery via ancestor/diff checks against injectable FAKE_* env vars.

nodes.name: is_stacked_pr_repo
nodes.summary: Detects whether a repo opts into stacked-PR workflows via a rollup workflow file, keyword mentions in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, or an agent-toolbox directory.

nodes.name: parse_stacked_args
nodes.summary: Parses gh:pr CLI arguments into STACK_MODE/STACK_BASE/ISSUE_NUMBER, rejecting the mutually-exclusive combination of --no-stack and --base.

nodes.name: _gh_pr_default_is_ancestor
nodes.summary: Checks whether a ref is a git ancestor of HEAD, using a real `git merge-base --is-ancestor` call or a FAKE_ANCESTOR_REFS stub in tests.

nodes.name: _gh_pr_default_default_tip_diff_check
nodes.summary: Determines whether a ref diverges from the default branch's tip by comparing merge-base results, so a candidate parent PR isn't mistaken for the default branch itself.

nodes.name: find_parent_pr_candidates
nodes.summary: Scans open PRs and filters to those whose head ref is an ancestor of HEAD and diverges from the default branch, producing a list of viable parent-PR candidates for stacking.

nodes.name: _gh_pr_default_parent_state
nodes.summary: Fetches a parent PR's state and body in a single combined `gh pr view` call, caching the body for reuse by the stacked-body guard.

nodes.name: _gh_pr_default_parent_body
nodes.summary: Returns a parent PR's body, preferring the cache populated by _gh_pr_default_parent_state to avoid a duplicate API call.

nodes.name: assert_parent_pr_open
nodes.summary: Fails with rc=5 and a remediation hint when the chosen parent PR is not in the OPEN state.

nodes.name: assert_parent_pr_not_stacked
nodes.summary: Fails with rc=6 when the parent PR's body itself declares a 'Depends on #N' line, since multi-level PR stacking is not supported.

nodes.name: helper_fallback_nf1.sh
nodes.summary: Bats test fixture mirroring the canonical F-2 helper-fallback pattern (NF-1 guarantee) reused across six gh:* skills: call the board-status-sync helper when present, silently skip when absent, never abort the caller.

nodes.name: nf1_canonical_block
nodes.summary: Executes the canonical F-2 block: sources the board-status-sync helper if readable and invokes it, otherwise continues without error (NF-1 guarantee).

nodes.name: nf1_install_fake_helper
nodes.summary: Writes a synthetic stub helper file (a fake _gh_project_status_sync) to a given path so tests can exercise the 'helper present' branch of nf1_canonical_block.

nodes.name: pace_control.sh
nodes.summary: Bats test fixture mirroring the gh-add-ai-metrics skill's pace-control helpers: duration parsing/formatting, budget checks, and ETA computation for paced write loops.

nodes.name: parse_duration
nodes.summary: Parses a compound duration string like '1h30m' into a total number of seconds.

nodes.name: format_duration
nodes.summary: Formats a number of seconds back into a compact 'XhYmZs' human-readable duration string.

nodes.name: compute_eta
nodes.summary: Estimates the time remaining for a paced sequence of writes given the write count and per-write pace interval.

nodes.name: claude_plugin_structure.sh
nodes.summary: Bats test fixture that is the executable, testable mirror of the claude-plugin-structure-check/refactor skills' M1-M10 mandatory checks, R1-R8 recommended checks, aggregate verdict, and refactor-apply logic for Claude Code plugin repos (single vs mono layout).
nodes.languageNotes: Kept in lockstep with structure-spec.md by convention comment — a markdown spec with no shell entry point, so this file is the only place the M/R rules are actually executable and bats-testable.

nodes.name: _cps_detect_mode
nodes.summary: Determines whether a plugin repo is 'single' or 'mono' layout, using an optional forced override, then marketplace.json's plugins[].source, then filesystem fallback checks.

nodes.name: _cps_plugin_roots
nodes.summary: Emits the plugin-root paths (relative to repo) for the detected mode: '.' for single mode with a manifest, or each plugins/<name> directory for mono mode.

nodes.name: cps_check_M3

nodes.languageNotes: Uses a workflow DSL (phase/agent/parallel/log helpers) rather than plain Node.js — each `agent()` call dispatches a long natural-language prompt to a sub-agent and awaits its text result, chaining five sequential phases.

nodes.name: harness-legacy-check.md
nodes.summary: Original Korean prompt/spec that requests a read-only harness-legacy-scan Dynamic Workflow, defining its audit scope, constraints (no file edits, no hook/MCP changes), and the five specialist-agent breakdown that harness-legacy-check.js implements.

nodes.name: design.md

nodes.name: design.md

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.name: design-C.md
nodes.summary: Final (design-C) specification for the ai-worktree:spawn skill: agent identification, auto-increment index allocation, branch creation strategy, functional/non-functional requirements, user scenarios, flowchart, failure handling, and teardown strategy, with an appendix consolidating three prior design drafts.

nodes.name: design.md
nodes.summary: Design for the write-task-history skill: requirements, data sources, JIRA-ticket and git-PR output formats, processing flow, draft SKILL.md structure, and nine settled design decisions (filename format, storage path, append separator, etc.).

nodes.name: example-output.md
nodes.summary: Concrete example of a task-history file produced by the write-task-history skill, showing the JIRA-ticket and PR sections for a sample dotfiles recovery task.

nodes.name: agentmemory.service
nodes.summary: systemd user-service unit that runs the agentmemory persistent memory server as a simple long-lived process, restarting on failure and logging to the systemd journal.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Setup playbook for agentmemory: installation, running it as an always-on systemd user service (referencing agentmemory.service), the cwd-relative data-storage pitfall, LLM/embedding key config, Claude Code MCP connection, and native skill/auto-capture plugin install.

nodes.name: kanban-board-setup.md
nodes.summary: Playbook for setting up a GitHub Projects kanban board: quick-start one-click script, a manual fallback using gh api calls (project creation, repo linking, status field options, PR template, built-in workflows), lifecycle notes, and a smoke-test verification section.

nodes.name: install-hooks.sh
nodes.summary: Installs the generic post-commit work-log hook (symlink to post-commit.generic) into a target project's .git/hooks/, supporting a --force overwrite flag, and prints guidance about this repo's own pre-push local-pytest policy.

nodes.name: post-commit
nodes.summary: Dotfiles-repo-specific post-commit hook that parses the last commit message (type/scope/TimeSpent/Category/Jira key) and appends a work-log entry, optionally auto-committing it into a separate playbook repo when DOTFILES_WORKLOG_ENABLED / DOTFILES_WORKLOG_PLAYBOOK_AUTOCOMMIT are set.

nodes.name: post-commit.generic
nodes.summary: Portable, project-agnostic version of the post-commit work-log hook meant to be symlinked into any project's .git/hooks/post-commit — parses commit metadata into work_log.txt and optionally auto-commits it into a configurable playbook repo (WORK_LOG_DIR / AUTO_COMMIT_REPO env overrides).

nodes.name: pre-commit
nodes.summary: Project-level pre-commit runner: sources git/config/hook-config.sh and every check module in git/hooks/checks/, enforces a protected-branch guard first, then runs shebang/naming/UX/subshell/pipe-loop/zsh-emulation/alias/wrapper/purity/shellcheck/gh-api-type/hardcoded-home-path checks against staged files, aggregating blocking violations vs warnings before deciding the exit code.

nodes.name: pre-push
nodes.summary: Git pre-push hook with three layers: a local pytest run (mise run test, once per push) replacing the CI test job, a protected-branch block per ref, and an opt-in upstream leak guard that scans commit messages and diffs in the push range against LEAK_PATTERNS_ERE when the push target matches UPSTREAM_REMOTES_ERE.

nodes.name: prepare-commit-msg
nodes.summary: Prepares a commit-message template by extracting a JIRA-style key from the current branch name (per TEMPLATE_BRANCHES/JIRA_PATTERN rules) and prepending it to the commit message file, skipping merge/squash/amend commits and branches whose message already has content.

nodes.name: commit-msg-validator.sh
nodes.summary: Reusable Conventional Commits validator library exposing validate_commit_message and its component checks (type, subject length, body length, multi-line format, forbidden patterns), consumed by git/hooks/commit-msg.

nodes.name: gh-credential-helper.sh
nodes.summary: Portable git credential helper wrapper that probes common gh CLI install locations (Linux system paths, Homebrew, user-local bin) before falling back to PATH, so git/.gitconfig's credential.helper works unmodified across machines.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Main git module setup script: ensures ~/.gitconfig includes the tracked SSOT via the include model (never a symlink, to survive `gh auth setup-git` rewrites), symlinks pre-commit/post-commit hooks into .git/hooks, installs the global pre-commit hook and sets core.hooksPath globally, and offers an SSH key generation/registration flow for GitHub.

nodes.name: test-pre-push.sh
nodes.summary: Regression test suite (T1-T7) for git/hooks/pre-push covering protected-branch precedence, upstream leak-guard detection in commit messages and diffs, and the SKIP_LEAK_GUARD/SKIP_PRE_PUSH escape hatches, using an isolated fake-repo test harness.

nodes.name: test-prepare-commit-msg.sh
nodes.summary: Test suite validating JIRA-key extraction and commit-message template generation logic used by git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.

nodes.name: test_hooks.sh
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the 2-tier git hook system: builds a throwaway repo wired with copies of the global and project pre-commit hooks plus checks/, then asserts each guardrail (forbidden .env files, tools/custom auto-sourcing, missing exec guards, wrong shebangs, library-purity violations, hardcoded home paths) blocks or allows the right staged changes.

nodes.name: _resolve_scan_range
nodes.summary: Resolves the commit range to scan for the leak guard, falling back to the merge-base against <remote>/main or master when pushing a brand-new branch, or just the tip commit if no tracking branch is found.

nodes.name: _leak_scan_range
nodes.summary: Scans a commit range's messages and added/modified file contents against LEAK_PATTERNS_ERE, failing open on git-plumbing errors, warning past 100 commits, and reporting every match found.

nodes.name: validate_no_forbidden_patterns
nodes.summary: Rejects commit messages containing forbidden literal patterns as part of Conventional Commits validation.

nodes.name: validate_commit_type
nodes.summary: Validates that the commit message's type prefix (feat, fix, docs, etc.) is one of the allowed Conventional Commits types.

nodes.name: validate_subject_length
nodes.summary: Checks that the commit subject line does not exceed the configured character limit.

nodes.name: validate_body_length
nodes.summary: Validates commit body line lengths against the configured wrap limit.

nodes.name: validate_multi_line_format
nodes.summary: Validates that a multi-line commit message has the expected blank-line separation between subject and body.

nodes.name: validate_commit_message
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that runs every commit-message validation sub-check (empty check, forbidden patterns, type, subject/body length, multi-line format) and aggregates the results; called by git/hooks/commit-msg.

nodes.name: create_symlink
nodes.summary: Creates a symlink from source to target, backing up any pre-existing real file or differing symlink first.

nodes.name: ensure_gitconfig_include
nodes.summary: Ensures ~/.gitconfig is a real machine-local file whose top section [include]s the tracked git/.gitconfig SSOT, preserving any existing local customizations instead of overwriting them.

nodes.name: register_ssh_key
nodes.summary: Adds a generated SSH key to the running ssh-agent so it's available for GitHub authentication.

nodes.name: generate_ssh_key
nodes.summary: Generates a new ed25519 SSH key for GitHub when the user has none, prompting for an email and passphrase.

nodes.name: configure_git_remote_ssh
nodes.summary: Rewrites the current repo's git remote URL to use SSH instead of HTTPS once SSH access to GitHub is confirmed.

nodes.name: setup_ssh_auto
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the automatic SSH setup flow — checks for an existing key, ensures the agent is running, generates/registers a key as needed, and verifies GitHub access.

nodes.name: run_test
nodes.summary: Generic test-runner helper that executes a pre-push hook test case and reports pass/fail with diagnostic output.

nodes.name: _setup_repo
nodes.summary: Builds an isolated temporary git repository fixture used by each pre-push test case.

nodes.name: _invoke_hook
nodes.summary: Invokes the pre-push hook under test with simulated stdin ref lines and captures its exit code and output.

nodes.name: _assert_eq
nodes.summary: Assertion helper comparing expected vs actual values and reporting a test failure with a diff-style message.

nodes.name: _t1_origin_push_with_pattern_passes
nodes.summary: T1: verifies a leak-pattern match on a non-upstream (origin) push is NOT blocked, since the leak guard only activates for upstream remotes.

nodes.name: _t2_upstream_push_no_pattern_passes
nodes.summary: T2: verifies a clean push to an upstream remote passes when no leak pattern is present.

nodes.name: _t3_upstream_push_pattern_in_commit_message_blocks
nodes.summary: T3: verifies an upstream push is blocked when a leak pattern appears in a commit message.

nodes.name: _t4_upstream_push_pattern_in_diff_blocks
nodes.summary: T4: verifies an upstream push is blocked when a leak pattern appears in file content added or modified in the push range.

nodes.name: _t5_skip_leak_guard_escape_hatch
nodes.summary: T5: verifies SKIP_LEAK_GUARD=1 bypasses the leak-guard layer while other checks still run.

nodes.name: _t6_skip_pre_push_escape_hatch
nodes.summary: T6: verifies SKIP_PRE_PUSH=1 bypasses the entire pre-push hook.

nodes.name: _t7_protected_branch_takes_priority
nodes.summary: T7: verifies the protected-branch block fires before, and independently of, the leak-guard layer when both conditions are met.

nodes.name: run_test
nodes.summary: Runs a single branch-name-to-JIRA-key extraction test case and compares the result against the expected value.

nodes.name: assert_failure
nodes.summary: Assertion helper expecting a command to fail; reports a test failure if it unexpectedly succeeds.

nodes.name: make_repo
nodes.summary: Builds a throwaway git repository fixture wired with copies of the global and project pre-commit hooks plus the checks/ directory, used as the harness for every hook-integration test case.

nodes.name: test_allows_spaces_in_filename
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook allows staging a filename containing spaces.

nodes.name: test_blocks_forbidden_env_file
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook blocks committing a forbidden .env file.

nodes.name: test_blocks_init_sourcing_tools_custom
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook blocks an init file that auto-sources tools/custom (the postmortem_init_check guardrail).

nodes.name: test_blocks_auto_exec_custom_script_without_guard
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook blocks an executable tools/custom script that lacks the required interactive guard.

nodes.name: test_blocks_custom_script_wrong_shebang
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook blocks a tools/custom script with an incorrect shebang.

nodes.name: test_blocks_library_purity_top_level_read
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook blocks a library file that performs a top-level (non-function-wrapped) read, violating library-purity rules.

nodes.name: test_blocks_library_purity_top_level_install
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook blocks a library file that performs a top-level install/side-effect statement.

nodes.name: test_allows_library_purity_commented_install
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook allows an install-like statement when it appears only inside a comment.

nodes.name: test_blocks_hardcoded_home_path_in_zshrc
nodes.summary: Verifies the pre-commit hook blocks a hardcoded absolute home path introduced in a zsh rc file (issue #737 guardrail).

nodes.name: test_allows_hardcoded_home_path_with_marker
nodes.summary: Verifies the hardcoded-home-path check allows an absolute path when it carries the explicit allow marker.

nodes.name: test_allows_home_var_reference
nodes.summary: Verifies the hardcoded-home-path check allows a $HOME variable reference instead of a literal path.

nodes.name: test_allows_preexisting_abs_home_on_unrelated_edit

nodes.name: product-requirements.md

Included (never symlinked) into the machine-local ~/.gitconfig by git/setup.sh.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md

nodes.name: gcp-scan-skip-paths.conf

nodes.name: gcp-scan-skip.conf
nodes.summary: Known-resolved commit-SHA skip list for `gcp scan` (issue #1039) so already-reconciled or permanently upstream-only commits stop re-triggering scan warnings.

nodes.name: pre-commit
nodes.summary: User-level global git pre-commit hook (wired via core.hooksPath) that runs lightweight universal safety checks — secret/key detection, conflict markers, forbidden filenames with git-crypt and .env allowlist awareness, trailing whitespace, debug-code and large-file warnings, and a work/personal email-vs-directory mismatch warning — then delegates to whichever project-specific hook it finds among .githooks, git/hooks, or .git/hooks.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately portable POSIX-ish bash (custom get_realpath fallback chain) so it behaves identically on macOS and Linux without relying on GNU-only coreutils.

nodes.name: wrapper_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check module providing check_wrapper_function, which flags wrapper-function anti-patterns — functions that merely forward to another command without adding value — as a non-blocking warning.

nodes.name: zsh_emulation_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check module providing check_zsh_emulation_issues, an informational check that flags zsh-compatibility issues in shared shell scripts.

nodes.name: commit-msg
nodes.summary: Project-level commit-msg git hook: runs the closing-keyword guard (hard-blocks Refs/Resolves/See in favor of Closes/Fixes) before delegating to commit-msg-validator.sh's validate_commit_message for Conventional Commits format checking, in warning-first mode by default (COMMIT_MSG_STRICT=1 to enforce, COMMIT_MSG_WARN_ONLY=1 to override a strict failure).

nodes.name: get_realpath
nodes.summary: Portable realpath resolver (macOS/Linux, with a manual symlink-following fallback) used to detect self-execution loops and to locate the sibling hook-config.sh relative to the hook's real path.

nodes.name: is_forbidden_staged_path
nodes.summary: Determines whether a staged path must block the commit — exempts git-crypt-managed files, then checks path-based rules (.aws/credentials), forbidden basenames (id_rsa, *.pem, credentials.json), and .env files against an example/sample/template/dist allowlist.

nodes.name: check_wrapper_function
nodes.summary: Scans a shell script for functions whose body is a thin pass-through wrapper around another command or function and reports each occurrence as a warning-level violation.

nodes.name: NuGet.Config.internal

nodes.name: AGENTS.md

nodes.name: obsidian

nodes.languageNotes: Written as a real executable rather than a shell function specifically so PATH resolution works across the non-interactive process boundary that AI coding agents use (shell functions are not inherited by child processes).

nodes.name: _ob_is_wsl

nodes.name: _ob_appimage_bin

nodes.name: setup.sh

nodes.name: opencode.json.external

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md frames this repository as opinionated Bash/Zsh dotfiles for reproducible terminal environments across WSL, Linux, and macOS. It lays out the four pillars the rest of this tour will walk through in code: environment-aware setup, modular shell configuration split by purpose, cross-shell shared code, and a built-in UX library with integrated linting. Keep this directory structure in mind — bash/, zsh/, shell-common/, git/ — as every following step drills into one of these pieces.

tour.title: Entry Point: setup.sh & install.sh
tour.description: setup.sh is the orchestrator a new user actually runs: it canonicalizes the dotfiles root and delegates to the bash/, zsh/, git/, and claude/ sub-setup scripts to create symlinks and export required environment variables. install.sh is the optional companion that runs afterward for IDE integration and Postgres config — it depends on setup.sh having already bootstrapped the shell environment. Together they are the README's 'Quick Start' made concrete: clone, run setup.sh, then optionally run install.sh.

tour.title: Cross-Shell Entry Points
tour.description: bash/main.bash and zsh/main.zsh are the actual runtime loaders sourced by ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc respectively — this is where setup.sh's symlinks lead at every new shell startup. bash/main.bash guards against loading under zsh, non-interactive shells, or Codex CLI before bootstrapping; zsh/main.zsh resolves DOTFILES_ROOT and then sources shell-common in a fixed phase order (env, aliases, functions, integrations) before its own zsh-only modules. This is the 'Cross-Shell Support' feature from the README: two thin, shell-specific loaders converging on one shared library.
tour.languageLesson: Every file that produces output starts with an interactive guard (`case $- in *i*) ;; *) return 0 ;; esac`) so sourcing it from a script or non-interactive subshell is a silent no-op — critical for a dotfiles repo where the same files get sourced by real shells, test harnesses, and CI alike.

tour.title: shell-common Core Bootstrap
tour.description: Following zsh/main.zsh's first sourcing calls (the natural dependency chain in this codebase) leads straight into shell-common's foundational utilities: safe_source.sh safely sources shell files with a success counter and distinguishes critical failures from optional ones; dotfiles_root.sh canonicalizes $DOTFILES_ROOT to the main git worktree so linked worktrees don't leave dangling symlinks; setup_mode.sh reads the ~/.dotfiles-setup-mode file that drives the Public/Internal/External environment detection the README advertises. These three are the plumbing every other shell-common module builds on.
tour.languageLesson: POSIX-compatible sourcing across bash and zsh forbids the common bash idiom `source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/file.sh"` (BASH_SOURCE doesn't exist in zsh) — this repo always sources via the resolved `${SHELL_COMMON}` absolute path instead, which is why dotfiles_root.sh's canonicalization matters so much.

tour.title: The UX Library Convention
tour.description: ux_lib.sh is the single POSIX-compliant styling dependency for every script in the repo — colors, headers, success/error/warning output, spinners, progress bars, prompts, and table formatting all live here, and it has the highest fan-out (17 outgoing references) of any shell-common file. The project convention is absolute: app scripts never call raw echo, printf, or tput directly, they call ux_lib functions like ux_header and ux_success. UX_GUIDELINES.md documents the design principles and color semantics behind it, so any new script written for this repo starts by sourcing this file.
tour.languageLesson: Terminal UX in POSIX shell means detecting capability rather than assuming it — ux_lib checks for color/tty support before emitting ANSI codes, so scripts degrade gracefully when piped or run in CI.

tour.title: Environment-Specific Configuration
tour.description: shell-common/setup.sh (called from step 2's root setup.sh) symlinks per-tool configuration files — pip, uv, cargo, nuget, npm, bun, opencode — into their canonical locations and resolves per-account settings like a Knox ID. proxy.local.example is a template for the gitignored override file it produces: a machine-specific proxy config that never gets committed. This is the README's 'Environment-Specific Setup' feature in action — the same setup.sh call produces different results depending on whether you're on a Public, Internal, or External machine.
tour.languageLesson: The `*.local.example` → gitignored `*.local.sh` pattern keeps machine-specific secrets and overrides (proxy hosts, API keys) out of version control while still giving every contributor a documented template to copy from.

tour.title: Adding a New Tool Integration
tour.description: init.sh has the highest fan-in of any file in the repo (21 other scripts depend on it) — it's sourced by every custom tool script to resolve DOTFILES_ROOT/SHELL_COMMON paths and load ux_lib exactly once, replacing what used to be hardcoded relative paths in 20+ places. claude.sh shows the pattern in practice: a full integration module handling install/init/settings for the Claude Code CLI. shell-common/AGENTS.md documents the required 3-step sequence for adding a new integration like this one — this is the file a contributor reads before adding support for a new tool.

tour.title: Git Hooks & Workflow
tour.description: git/hooks/pre-commit is the project-level hook runner: it sources hook-config.sh (the single source of truth for secret-detection regexes, forbidden filenames, and expected shebangs) and every check module in git/hooks/checks/, enforcing a protected-branch guard first and then a dozen-plus checks — shellcheck, naming, UX-library usage, subshell sourcing — before deciding pass/fail. shared.sh is the common helper those check modules call to materialize staged file content for scanning. git/AGENTS.md ties it together as the 2-tier hook system referenced in the top-level CLAUDE.md, debuggable via GIT_HOOKS_DEBUG=1.

tour.title: The Test Suite
tour.description: tests/test is the single entrypoint the project's `mise run test` invokes: it orchestrates bats shell-unit tests in parallel, golden-rules static analysis, and pytest integration tests. test_helper.bash has the highest fan-in in the whole graph (22 dependents) — every .bats file sources it to freeze the real DOTFILES_ROOT/SHELL_COMMON and get HOME isolation before exercising bash/main.bash and zsh/main.zsh from steps 3-4. conftest.py plays the same role for the Python side, spawning isolated shell subprocesses against a temp HOME for every test_*.py file.
tour.languageLesson: bats tests are POSIX shell files with `@test "description" { ... }` blocks; pytest's conftest.py fixtures are the Python-side equivalent of test_helper.bash — both exist to give tests a hermetic HOME so they never touch a real dotfiles installation.

tour.title: CI/CD and the mise Task SSOT
tour.description: ci.yml runs mise-driven lint (ruff, mypy, shellcheck, shfmt) on every push and PR to main or develop — notably, the old pytest CI job was deliberately moved to the local pre-push git hook from step 8 to save CI runner time, a real architectural decision worth noticing. test.yml is a leaner shell-lint-only workflow. Both trigger against mise.toml, which is the actual SSOT for every `mise run lint`/`fix`/`test` task referenced throughout this repo's CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md files — CI and local dev run the exact same task definitions.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows declare `on` triggers, `jobs`, and `steps`; keeping the actual lint/test logic in mise.toml tasks rather than inline YAML means CI and a developer's terminal can never drift out of sync.

tour.title: Claude Code Integration Layer
tour.description: claude/setup.sh symlinks settings.json, the statusline script, skills, and docs into ~/.claude (or per-account config dirs), handling legacy-layout migrations along the way — it's one of the four sub-setups step 2's root setup.sh delegates to. claude/AGENTS.md documents the 4-way entry-level symlink scheme shared across Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Gemini CLI, plus why settings.json is copied as a real file rather than symlinked (so `/model` writes don't dirty the tracked source of truth). This is a whole architectural layer in this repo — dotfiles that configure not just your shell, but your AI coding tools too.

tour.title: Design History and Conventions
tour.description: REFACTORING_SETUP_SH.md is the project's own record of refactoring setup.sh (step 2) toward SOLID principles — config extraction, function separation, an environments.conf SSOT — showing this codebase treats its own architecture as documented, evolving history rather than static code. command-design-pattern.md closes the loop from the other direction: it's the SSOT new shell commands must follow (naming, dispatcher types, help integration), the same convention discipline you've now seen enforced by the UX library, the git hooks, and the test suite throughout this tour.
